027c83a1-57b5-4be4-b827-741b8e85712a.title: Kickoff agent issue creates are local-only — need automatic sync to hub
027c83a1-57b5-4be4-b827-741b8e85712a.description: Issues created by kickoff agents in worktrees (via crosslink quick/create) are written to the worktree's local issues.db but never synced back to the hub branch. The kickoff prompt (kickoff.rs:837) claims the db is shared via crosslink/hub, but in practice init_worktree_agent() creates a fresh db (via crosslink init --force) and sync only hydrates FROM the hub. Agent writes don't flow back because git push is blocked. Fix: kickoff agents should automatically sync their issue mutations to the hub branch, either via crosslink sync at regular intervals or by writing directly to issue files on the hub branch.

03dddcfc-dac5-4fc6-b003-c8b47bcc3dc7.title: Test: offline-simultaneous v3 bootstrap convergence (GH#629 analog)
03dddcfc-dac5-4fc6-b003-c8b47bcc3dc7.description: Residual analog of GH#629 noted while closing it: two machines bootstrapping v3 hubs while BOTH offline against the same remote produce independent genesis commits on refs/crosslink/checkpoint and refs/crosslink/meta. Expected-benign by design: agent refs never collide (distinct names); checkpoint converges because both geneses carry the identical fixed-epoch sentinel watermark and push with force-with-lease (lease loser refetches and adopts); meta divergence surfaces as a reported NonFastForward, and meta is informational (state derives from agent refs + checkpoint). Add a two-machine test mirroring test_two_machine_v3_join_round_trip but with BOTH sides bootstrapping before either pushes: assert post-reconnect convergence to identical reduced state on both machines, no error escalation, and document the meta-ref disposition (which genesis wins and why it does not matter). If the test reveals the meta non-fast-forward blocks anything, fix by adopting the remote meta unconditionally (writer-authoritative pattern).

041f0d12-b43c-4b3f-8fdd-adda0eb80aa7.title: Fix GH#583: entrypoint init --force overwrites hook-config.json on every container start

44adf870-9c58-4db1-b295-fd013d044b38.author: dashboard-bootstrap
44adf870-9c58-4db1-b295-fd013d044b38.content: Two surfaces drop --force: (1) crosslink/resources/container/entrypoint.sh:131 — container's init call inside the agent's container; (2) crosslink/src/commands/kickoff/launch.rs:439 — host-side init_worktree_agent that runs against a freshly-created worktree before the container starts. Both were the same bug pattern and the issue's symptom requires BOTH to be fixed (host runs first, then container runs again — either one re-templates). Plain 'crosslink init' short-circuits at mod.rs:751 when .crosslink/ and .claude/ both exist (the common case for any worktree checked out from a branch that committed them), so the init becomes a no-op. Kept --skip-signing and --defaults on the host call to suppress the TUI walkthrough on the rare path where init actually has work to do. cargo fmt clean, clippy clean, kickoff tests 149/149 pass.

08f6aaa4-bb1e-4d66-9463-5c7acf8f8bf9.title: Resolve crosslink binary via PATH first, not current_exe

abd9eead-936d-4908-973a-a237a1d5a11b.author: anon-ab6221f8
abd9eead-936d-4908-973a-a237a1d5a11b.content:
Shipped in 3576eae8. User called out the anti-pattern correctly — self-exe pin meant 'cargo install crosslink' didn't actually update dashboard subprocess behaviour. Now resolve_crosslink_bin() prefers PATH (canonical installed shape), falls back to self-exe only for dev workflows, with CROSSLINK_BIN env override for advanced cases.

Also fixed the FK constraint bug in untrack_with_path that was blocking cleanup of orphaned rows: the actions audit table FK to projects(id) had no ON DELETE clause, so projects with any dashboard-initiated writes (close/comment/lock/etc.) couldn't be untracked at all. Now NULLs out actions.project_id in a transaction before dropping the project row — audit history preserved as orphan rows.

Grid is now clean: dollspace-gay/OpenClaudia, dollspace-gay/ferrotorch, forecast-bio/crosslink. Ready for the user to re-add forecast-bio repos via the new Clone & Track form (which defaults to $HOME).

14344570-e120-4e79-b327-76457f6e2874.title: Fix agent signing setup fragility during initial repo initialization

2b3265c6-d4bc-4803-8e3a-2d0d1e2afb1e.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-signing
2b3265c6-d4bc-4803-8e3a-2d0d1e2afb1e.content:
Plan: Fix the three related bugs in agent signing initialization:

1. **Key never published when hub doesn't exist**: In agent init, publish_agent_key() silently returns Ok(()) when hub cache isn't initialized. After sync creates the hub, nobody re-publishes. Fix: Add ensure_agent_key_published() to SyncManager, call it from sync_cmd after init_cache.

2. **Bootstrap ordering**: In bootstrap(), configure_signing() (step 8) enables commit.gpgsign=true BEFORE publish_agent_key() (step 9). Step 9 then tries to commit with signing on, which can fail. Fix: Swap order — publish key first, then configure signing.

3. **commit_trust_change uses signed commits during bootstrap**: The key-publishing commit should not require signing since signing isn't fully set up yet. Fix: Use -c commit.gpgsign=false for key-publishing commits via a new commit_trust_change_unsigned() path.

Additionally: Improve error messages when publish_agent_key() skips due to missing hub cache.

dccbf4f5-0601-4813-8c84-411249e4a7e5.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-signing
dccbf4f5-0601-4813-8c84-411249e4a7e5.content:
Plan: Fix the three related bugs in agent signing initialization:

1. **Key never published when hub doesn't exist**: In agent init, publish_agent_key() silently returns Ok(()) when hub cache isn't initialized. After sync creates the hub, nobody re-publishes. Fix: Add ensure_agent_key_published() to SyncManager, call it from sync_cmd after init_cache.

2. **Bootstrap ordering**: In bootstrap(), configure_signing() (step 8) enables commit.gpgsign=true BEFORE publish_agent_key() (step 9). Step 9 then tries to commit with signing on, which can fail. Fix: Swap order — publish key first, then configure signing.

3. **commit_trust_change uses signed commits during bootstrap**: The key-publishing commit should not require signing since signing isn't fully set up yet. Fix: Use -c commit.gpgsign=false for key-publishing commits via a new commit_trust_change_unsigned() path.

Additionally: Improve error messages when publish_agent_key() skips due to missing hub cache.

6fbc2cb4-3bd2-4391-8545-7e1c6135f11d.author: anon-ab6221f8
6fbc2cb4-3bd2-4391-8545-7e1c6135f11d.content: Change LocksFile.locks from HashMap<String, Lock> to HashMap<i64, Lock>. Add serde deserialize_with to parse string keys from JSON into i64. Remove all .to_string() conversions in accessors.

d0791211-4073-44fc-95ac-8abd832e6c63.author: anon-ab6221f8
d0791211-4073-44fc-95ac-8abd832e6c63.content: This is a larger refactor than originally scoped. The HashMap<String, Lock> is used in LocksFile which is serialized/deserialized to JSON on the hub branch. Changing to HashMap<i64, Lock> requires custom serde for backward compatibility with existing locks.json files that have string keys. Also need to update all sites that insert/remove from the map. Checking scope.

1. **global.md** — Add a 'Blocked Actions' section documenting commands blocked by work-check.py (git push/merge/rebase/reset/etc.), gated commands (git commit requires active issue), and directing agents to ask the user for manual steps instead of attempting blocked commands. 2. **kickoff.md** — Add lint/format check instructions to the agent prompt template. In step 8 (test suite), also include running the project's linter and formatter (cargo clippy/fmt, eslint, ruff, etc.) before committing. This ensures subagents always lint before marking work complete.

- crosslink/resources/crosslink/rules/global.md (blocked actions documentation)
- crosslink/resources/claude/commands/kickoff.md (lint instructions in agent prompt)

1edb67d0-496e-4d3f-aa4f-31fbf8a6b73f.title: Fix TOCTOU race in hub lock allowing dual lock acquisition

2fdad551-714e-4597-93e6-5c121aed8f1c.author: anon-ab6221f8
2fdad551-714e-4597-93e6-5c121aed8f1c.content: Refactored hub lock: extracted try_create_lock helper, HubWriteLock now holds File handle (OS releases on crash). Stale lock removal is immediately followed by re-acquire in same iteration (no continue gap). Timeout force-removal also immediately re-acquires. All 1665 tests pass.

49e0ee37-3976-4fe7-92e1-09d5911b7a14.author: anon-ab6221f8
49e0ee37-3976-4fe7-92e1-09d5911b7a14.content: Replace PID-based create-delete dance with flock-style advisory locking using fs2 or holding the file handle open as the guard. If neither is available, use atomic create_new with proper error handling.

73418765-3ec0-430d-a568-d886a93301ea.author: anon-ab6221f8
73418765-3ec0-430d-a568-d886a93301ea.content: Chosen approach: after detecting stale PID, remove the lock and immediately attempt create_new in the same iteration, instead of continue-ing back to the top of the loop. This closes the TOCTOU window between remove and re-acquire. Also hold the File handle in HubWriteLock so the OS auto-releases on crash.

1fbbad1e-59d7-42e9-b484-42eef678a712.title: [sentinel-qa] TEST-ONLY — DO NOT ACTION: check blank line consistency in crosslink --help output (PR #553 QA)
1fbbad1e-59d7-42e9-b484-42eef678a712.description:
Sentinel signal: GH#560:replicate

**This issue exists only to exercise the sentinel replicate dispatcher for PR #553 QA. Will be closed once verification is complete — DO NOT action, DO NOT merge any agent output.**

## Observed (possibly)

It looks like `crosslink --help` may have inconsistent blank-line spacing between the `Commands:` section and the `Options:` section — sometimes one blank line, sometimes two. Purely cosmetic.

## Expected

Consistent single blank line between each section of the help output.

## Agent task (replicate only)

1. Capture `crosslink --help` stdout
2. Write a test in `tests/` that parses section boundaries and asserts consistent spacing
3. Run the test and report whether it fails or passes
4. If the test passes (no inconsistency), record a `resolution` comment noting the issue could not be reproduced

Either outcome — a failing test or a clean "cannot reproduce" — demonstrates the sentinel dispatch mechanism works.

## Severity

Cosmetic only. No production impact.

1ff72951-7cb1-40a1-a24b-a6c52ae27c50.title: Dashboard: make alerts clickable to expand with actions

bddb4119-d10c-429f-a05a-141b8272fccc.author: anon-ab6221f8
bddb4119-d10c-429f-a05a-141b8272fccc.content:
Shipped in a33f20f5. Whole alert row now clickable (+ Enter/Space keyboard); expansion surfaces kind-specific actions:

• lock:<id>: Release / Steal
• agent:<id>: Pause / Resume / Kill (via the git-native agent request protocol)
• issue:<id>: Close + inline Comment textarea (overdue_issue, orphan_subissue)
• project:<slug> / commit:<sha> / no-subject: Open project → link only

Reuses all existing write-surface hooks so audit/signing/hub-push behave identically to the project-detail flow. Project link in the summary + action buttons stopPropagation so clicks on them don't also toggle expansion. Local-only issues (UUID, no display_id) hide the action buttons since the CLI write surface only accepts numeric IDs.

Accessibility: role=button + aria-expanded + aria-label per row. parseSubjectRef exported for tests. 14 new vitest cases (parseSubjectRef edge cases, collapsed/expanded, keyboard toggle, each kind's action set, subject-less fallback, local-only hide, link-doesn't-toggle); full suite 95/95.

22609972-a1f7-4b46-8140-04ae255b43eb.title: hub-v3 PR3.5: emit events for ALL mutations; add comment/time-entry event variants
22609972-a1f7-4b46-8140-04ae255b43eb.description: Discovered during the #752 soak bring-up: production code emits events ONLY from shared_writer/locks.rs (lock claim/release/steal). The other 11 event variants (IssueCreated, IssueUpdated, StatusChanged, labels, deps, relations, milestones, parent) are reduced by compaction but never emitted -- all issue mutations travel the V2 JSON-file path. Comments and time entries have NO event variants at all. PR4 (delete the v2 file-write machinery, #754) is impossible until: (1) every mutation in shared_writer/mutations.rs emits its corresponding event through emit_compact_push (or its hub_v3 successor); (2) new event variants exist for CommentAdded and TimeEntryAdded (design doc needs amending -- the materialized issue JSON becomes a pure compaction cache only if comments are in the event stream); (3) compaction reduces the new variants; (4) dual-write soak coverage then extends to all mutation types, restarting the soak clock for the new paths. Does NOT block #753 (genesis migration replays materialized state, not event history).

1f92bf8e-ee57-49c9-87e4-ee83810d5cc0.author: dashboard-bootstrap
1f92bf8e-ee57-49c9-87e4-ee83810d5cc0.content: Emission wiring complete. Choke point: added events: Vec<Event> field to WriteSet; write_commit_push appends per-attempt via new append_envelopes() helper (the single hub_v3 mirror site) and stages agents/<id>/events.log in the SAME commit. emit_compact_push refactored onto the helper. Retry: append is inside the loop so recover_from_push_conflict's reset --hard HEAD~1 discards the committed+working-tree log line and the next prepare re-emits — no double-append. rewrite_as_offline rewrites the trailing IssueCreated line to display_id:None to match the offline file; promote_offline_issues rewrites it to the new claim. TIME ENTRIES: SQLite-only (timer.rs -> db.start/stop_timer); never written to hub, so TimeEntryAdded is intentionally not wired. All 17 hub mutations wired. Gates: fmt clean, both clippy gates clean, lib 1965 / bin 2954 / cli_integration 194 all green.

4c9980bb-f77c-4533-a2a6-384fe7334c5b.author: dashboard-bootstrap
4c9980bb-f77c-4533-a2a6-384fe7334c5b.content: Committed on feature/756-full-event-emission: all mutations event-sourced, 8 new/extended variants, reducer rules with id adoption + tombstones, single emission choke point with dual-write mirror. Director review added on top of agent passes: claim-rewrite signature invalidation fix (clear + re-sign) and shadow-ref byte-parity rebuild via new hub_v3::commit_log_bytes -- both were silent-divergence bugs the agents missed. Gates: fmt, both clippy profiles, 1966 lib + 2955 bin + 194 integration green. Awaiting push.

82514b7b-c595-4393-b3d9-a897d2d166fe.author: dashboard-bootstrap
82514b7b-c595-4393-b3d9-a897d2d166fe.content: Design from mutation inventory (17 non-emitting mutations across mutations.rs/milestones.rs; only locks emit). NEW/EXTENDED EVENT VARIANTS (all serde-additive): CommentAdded (mirrors CommentEntry fields + issue_uuid + comment display_id claimed from counters), TimeEntryAdded (mirrors TimeEntry + entry identity for dedupe), IssueDeleted (tombstone), MilestoneCreated/MilestoneClosed/MilestoneDeleted (mirror milestone file fields + claimed display_id), ScheduleChanged (full-state LWW: issue_uuid + scheduled_at + due_at as post-change values), IssueCreated gains scheduled_at/due_at AND display_id: Option<i64> (the counter-claimed id -- reducer adopts carried ids first-claim-wins by OrderingKey, allocates above frozen max only for None; closes the transition window where event-derived ids could disagree with file-written ids and clobber at materialization). REDUCER: CheckpointState gains milestones map, deleted-issue tombstone set; CompactIssue gains comments + time_entries (idempotent by uuid). Tombstones prevent resurrection by late events. MATERIALIZE: unchanged for comments/time-entries/milestones in this PR (files still written directly by mutations; reducer carries the data so the v3 read path has it; materialization becomes sole writer only at #754 cutover). EMISSION: new shared append_envelope helper centralizing v2 log append + hub_v3 dual-write mirror + shadow stats (moves the PR2 mirror hook so ALL events flow through it); write_commit_push gains event support so each mutation's event lands in the SAME commit as its file write (no second commit/push per op); emit_compact_push refactors onto the helper; all 17 mutations wired. Implementation split: core semantics (events+checkpoint+reducer) first, then emission wiring.

2614d561-77b1-4b5f-b6f9-fd670d5242a4.title: hub-v3 PR1: HubSource read abstraction over worktree files and object store
2614d561-77b1-4b5f-b6f9-fd670d5242a4.description: First PR of the hub v3 migration (.design/hub-v3-per-agent-refs.md, REQ-3). Introduce a HubSource trait that the materialization/reduction path reads through, with two impls: the current worktree-file reader and a git object-store reader (cat-file against refs). Proves the existing compaction reducer produces identical state from both sources before any write-path changes. Acceptance: reducer parity test over a populated hub via both impls; no behavior change for users.

05e5c235-252d-4411-8c1f-483eade65ea9.author: dashboard-bootstrap
05e5c235-252d-4411-8c1f-483eade65ea9.content: Design: new module crosslink/src/hub_source.rs with trait HubSource { agent_ids(), read_events(agent_id, after: Option<&OrderingKey>), read_checkpoint(), read_legacy_watermark(), allowed_signers_file() -> Option<PathBuf> }. Two impls: WorktreeSource (wraps cache_dir, byte-for-byte current read behavior) and ObjectStoreSource (repo dir + refname, reads committed tree via git ls-tree/cat-file with NO checkout; extracts allowed_signers blob to an owned TempDir because verify_event_signature shells to ssh-keygen with a path). Pure reduction extracted from compact() as reduce(&dyn HubSource) -> ReductionOutcome { state, changed_issues, changed_locks, events_processed }, including the watermark-none full-reset and empty-events early-return semantics. compact() becomes reduce(WorktreeSource) + unchanged write side (materialize, skew detection, write_checkpoint). events.rs gains from-bytes parse variants; path versions become wrappers. Parity test: populate hub layout in temp git repo, commit to branch, run reduce via both sources, assert identical CheckpointState via serde equality across scenarios (multi-agent, lock contention, incremental with watermark, full compaction).

295de578-8b6c-4963-95f1-89b72cc58fbc.title: crosslink dashboard discover: filesystem autodiscovery of crosslink-enabled repos (GH #429 followup)

70272941-543d-4923-a607-388dc153af69.author: anon-ab6221f8
70272941-543d-4923-a607-388dc153af69.content:
Shipped in c7d9683a. `crosslink dashboard discover [--root

...] [--depth N] [--track]` walks a bounded set of dev roots, matches directories that have BOTH `.git` and `.crosslink/` at the root, skips the usual noise list (node_modules/target/.venv/.cache/hidden dirs), and reports a SLUG/TRACKED?/PATH table. With --track it promotes new hits via the existing projects::track entry point. Symlink-loop defence via canonical-path visited-set; doesn't follow symlinks. 5 unit tests in a temp-dir fixture. Verified against the user's $HOME: found 3 repos (forecast-bio/crosslink, dollspace-gay/OpenClaudia, dollspace-gay/ferrotorch), --track promoted the two new ones, all three tiles populated on the live dashboard within one poll tick.

794fac53-e2e0-4b4a-82df-6bb70ffc82cf.author: anon-ab6221f8
794fac53-e2e0-4b4a-82df-6bb70ffc82cf.content:
Plan — fix my bad take from earlier. Local-fs discovery IS cheap: crosslink-enabled repos have a .crosslink/ directory at the repo root (and/or a crosslink/hub branch in the git remote — but local signal alone is enough and works offline). The prior 'can't tell which' was wrong; it was conflating 'scan the whole filesystem' with 'check a sensible list of dev roots'.

CLI surface: new subcommand
  crosslink dashboard discover [--root <PATH>]... [--depth <N>] [--track]

Behaviour:
- Walk each --root (default: $HOME) up to --depth directories deep (default 4).
- Skip obvious noise dirs: .git inside, node_modules, target, dist, .venv, __pycache__, .cache, Library, AppData, any dir starting with '.' except .crosslink itself.
- For every dir that is (a) a git repo root and (b) has .crosslink/ present, record a hit.
- Derive slug from origin remote URL; fall back to 'local/<basename>' for remote-less repos.
- Print hits as a table: SLUG / PATH / TRACKED?
- With --track, track every hit that isn't already tracked.

Reuses crate::dashboard::projects::track for the tracking path so the derivation logic stays identical. No GitHub round-trip — purely local-fs.

Tests: a temp-dir fixture mimicking a nested layout with 3 crosslink repos + 2 non-crosslink git repos + one node_modules; asserts exactly the 3 hits found, respects --depth bound, handles symlinks defensively (don't follow).

e3e540f7-57fb-463a-933f-37298cde75f0.content:
Design decisions from interview:
1. Tip-only view, no pager — show fork-point to branch tip only
2. Custom ASCII renderer reading raw git data + discover_agents() — not git log --graph passthrough
3. --svg deferred to follow-up
4. No ahead/behind counts or commit hashes — topology only, with unlabeled intermediate commits
5. --all scans git branches too, labels orphans with [orphan]
6. Annotations: tmux session name (most actionable), docker container, or status. Branch name always visible.
7. Command name: 'graph' per GH-491 comment

aa6086c9-abf8-41d1-a535-43ad31c133e8.author: anon-ab6221f8
aa6086c9-abf8-41d1-a535-43ad31c133e8.content:
## What

Implement the 4-layer deduplication system that prevents sentinel from acting on items already filed or already looked at. This is the most critical correctness component — without it, sentinel creates duplicate work on every poll cycle.

### Layer 1: Source-Level Filtering
Already handled in GitHubLabelSource (#652) — only returns issues that currently have the label. If a human removes the label, the issue vanishes from the next poll.

### Layer 2: SeenSet (In-Memory Dedup Cache)
Loaded once per sentinel cycle from sentinel_dispatches.

Key data structures:
- `SeenRecord`: disposition, outcome, attempt_number, model_used, completed_at
- `SeenSet`: HashMap<String, SeenRecord> keyed by signal_ref
- `SignalDecision`: enum { New, Escalate, Skip(&'static str) }

The `evaluate()` method determines what to do with each signal:
- "pending" -> Skip (agent in-flight)
- "success" -> Skip (already resolved)  
- "exhausted" -> Skip (both attempts failed)
- "failure"/"timeout" with attempt < 2 AND cooldown elapsed -> Escalate (retry with Opus)
- "failure"/"timeout" with attempt >= 2 -> Skip (max attempts reached)
- "orphaned" with attempt < 2 -> Escalate (worktree was cleaned up, retry)
- Not in SeenSet -> New (first dispatch)

### Layer 3: Database Constraint
Before inserting a new dispatch, query:
```sql
SELECT id, outcome, attempt_number FROM sentinel_dispatches
WHERE gh_issue_number = ? AND label = ?
ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1
```
This is the authoritative check that survives process restarts.

### Layer 4: GH Comment Dedup
Before posting to a GH issue, check for an existing comment containing the marker string "sentinel #<dispatch-id>". Uses `gh issue view <N> --json comments`. Prevents duplicate comments if sentinel crashes between posting and recording the outcome.

### Why 4 layers
- Layer 1 is passive (relies on GitHub state)
- Layer 2 is fast (in-memory, no I/O)
- Layer 3 is authoritative (survives restarts)
- Layer 4 is defensive (handles crash-between-post-and-record)

Each layer catches failures the previous one misses. Belt and suspenders.

### Testing
- SeenSet unit tests for every SignalDecision path
- Cooldown boundary tests (exactly at cooldown, 1 second before, 1 second after)
- DB constraint test: insert duplicate signal_ref, verify second attempt returns existing record
- GH comment dedup test: mock gh output with/without marker string

### Dependencies
- #651 (schema — sentinel_dispatches table)
- #652 (source — signals flow into SeenSet)

bcc9eb03-bd41-4625-8730-860ec6530936.author: dashboard-bootstrap
bcc9eb03-bd41-4625-8730-860ec6530936.content: Part 1 (v3 read source + version detection) implemented on feature/753-hub-v3-migrate. hub_v3.rs: CHECKPOINT_REF/META_REF consts; commit machinery generalized onto commit_single_file_tree with CasExpectation (MustNotExist/MustMatch/CurrentTip); commit_blob_to_ref + commit_files_to_ref (multi-file mktree, defensively sorted); push_ref/push_ref_with_lease (push_agent_ref now a thin wrapper); HubVersion + detect_hub_version + detect_remote_hub_version (single ls-remote, unreachable remote = hard error); HubMeta + read_hub_meta. hub_source.rs: RefHubSource pinned at construction (checkpoint/meta/all agent tips), events.log at tree root, vanished-commit hard error, allowed_signers from META_REF via owned TempDir. 12 new tests incl. keystone reduce(WorktreeSource)==reduce(RefHubSource) equivalence. Gates: fmt clean, both clippy gates clean, lib 1978 pass (baseline 1966+12), bin 2967 pass (baseline 2955+12). mktree finding: git 2.54 sorts unsorted stdin itself (identical tree SHA either way); sort kept defensively for older gits.

bd372e47-8db2-4331-be17-fc78b13ab403.author: dashboard-bootstrap
bd372e47-8db2-4331-be17-fc78b13ab403.content: Design (grounded): MIGRATE FLOW -- (1) preflight: fetch + force compaction, refuse on pending offline promotions; (2) build genesis CheckpointState FROM FILES (authoritative): all issue files + comment files + milestone files + counters -> issues/comments/time_entries (CompactIssue now carries them post-#756), display_id_map from file claims, next_* reconciled to on-disk maxima, locks from compacted state, watermark = max OrderingKey across all agent logs so v3 readers apply only post-genesis events; (3) seed refs/crosslink/agents/<id> from each v2 events.log via hub_v3::commit_log_bytes (child-commit reconcile when dual-write shadow refs already exist); (4) genesis checkpoint state.json onto refs/crosslink/checkpoint via generalized commit_named_blob helper; (5) refs/crosslink/meta: hub marker (hub_version=3, source commit) + allowed_signers; (6) push all refs fast-forward; (7) AC-6 GATE: full-state diff -- file-derived state vs reduce(RefHubSource) over the new refs; any field mismatch = FAIL + rollback (delete created refs; v2 branch never touched). NEW COMPONENT RefHubSource: HubSource impl over checkpoint ref + per-agent refs (becomes the #754 production read path; built here because the migration verification needs it). FINALIZE: separate explicit --finalize deletes crosslink/hub local+remote after re-verification -- this is the only hard stop for OLD binaries: grounding finding, read_layout_version callers use >=2 / unwrap_or(1), so already-shipped clients CANNOT be made to refuse a v3 hub via version bumps; v2 branch therefore remains authoritative-compatible until finalize, and mixed-version operation pre-finalize is documented as user-managed. v3-aware detection (hub_v3::hub_version -> V2/V3/Both) warns when operating v2-mode on a migrated hub; full refusal flips at #754.

33710ef7-869f-4a1c-83ea-d36362bde771.title: Fix kickoff agent key not being published after creation

21bdecf0-034d-4c4e-b7a9-a9a696e29f74.author: driver--fix-kickoff-agent-key-not-being-published-after-creation
21bdecf0-034d-4c4e-b7a9-a9a696e29f74.content: Plan: When kickoff calls agent::init with no_key=true, the new agent config has no ssh_public_key, so publish_agent_key is never called and ensure_agent_key_published also skips (no key in config). Fix: in init_worktree_agent (kickoff.rs), after creating the agent config with no_key=true, copy the parent's SSH key info into the new agent config, then call publish_agent_key to publish the parent's public key under the new agent ID.

26907f8e-3576-4d53-abbd-5434eccfa146.author: driver--fix-kickoff-agent-key-not-being-published-after-creation
26907f8e-3576-4d53-abbd-5434eccfa146.content: Decision: Fix in kickoff.rs init_worktree_agent rather than changing agent::init behavior. The no_key flag is also used by bootstrap and CLI, so changing its semantics could break other flows. Instead, kickoff should explicitly copy parent key info and publish after init.

5e6416fc-9f23-4ef6-890e-fd6a8c693b50.author: driver--fix-kickoff-agent-key-not-being-published-after-creation
5e6416fc-9f23-4ef6-890e-fd6a8c693b50.content: Plan: When kickoff calls agent::init with no_key=true, the new agent config has no ssh_public_key, so publish_agent_key is never called and ensure_agent_key_published also skips (no key in config). Fix: in init_worktree_agent (kickoff.rs), after creating the agent config with no_key=true, copy the parent's SSH key info into the new agent config, then call publish_agent_key to publish the parent's public key under the new agent ID.

8427452d-66c7-4770-8419-908ae77d72d1.author: driver--fix-kickoff-agent-key-not-being-published-after-creation

3df560a2-df46-40ac-bd0d-aafe2d725a3e.title: Add TUI startup sync, periodic background sync, and manual refresh keybinding

59de5317-4346-43d7-8bbc-f3b35b7ce8b1.author: maxine--tui-startup-sync
59de5317-4346-43d7-8bbc-f3b35b7ce8b1.content: Decision: Made 'r' a global keybinding that triggers full sync (fetch + hydrate) rather than keeping per-tab local refresh. ':r' command palette remains as local-only disk refresh. Added force_refresh() to Tab trait so sync completion can reload data for eagerly-loaded tabs (IssuesTab, KnowledgeTab). Tabs with on_enter refresh (AgentsTab, ConfigTab, MilestonesTab) use default force_refresh (on_leave + on_enter).

6fa7c828-41c6-4a00-8548-f192705bf129.author: maxine--tui-startup-sync
6fa7c828-41c6-4a00-8548-f192705bf129.content: Plan: Three sync features for the TUI (REQ-3, AC-7/AC-8/AC-9): (1) Startup sync in run() before App::new() — SyncManager::fetch() + hydrate_to_sqlite() with 'Syncing...' message before alternate screen. (2) Periodic 30s background sync via mpsc channel (same pattern as agents_tab). (3) Global 'r' keybinding intercepted before tab dispatch triggers full sync. Per-tab 'r' handlers delegate to global. ':r' command palette remains as local disk refresh. Key files: tui/mod.rs (main), plus all 5 tab files.

858e44a3-925a-41f1-b696-bfda1824b29a.author: maxine--plan-050
858e44a3-925a-41f1-b696-bfda1824b29a.content: Covers REQ-3, AC-7 through AC-9. Three parts: (1) startup sync before App::new() (~30 lines), (2) periodic 30s background sync using mpsc channel pattern from agents_tab.rs (~80 lines), (3) global 'r' keybinding for manual sync+refresh (~20 lines). No tab currently uses bare 'r'. SyncManager can run on background thread safely since hub cache worktree is separate from working tree.

a7cf7810-c7be-4bfc-bf25-4c94fe37f686.author: maxine--tui-startup-sync
a7cf7810-c7be-4bfc-bf25-4c94fe37f686.content: Result: Implemented all 3 sync features (AC-7, AC-8, AC-9). Startup sync: mod.rs:665-671, fetch+hydrate before alternate screen with 'Syncing...' message. Periodic sync: mod.rs:692-694, checks elapsed > 30s each loop tick, spawns background fetch. Manual refresh: mod.rs:213-215, global 'r' calls start_background_sync(). On sync completion poll_sync() hydrates DB and calls force_refresh() on active tab. 6 files changed, 151 insertions, 52 deletions. All 186 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Commit d60cfec on branch feature/add-tui-startup-sync-periodic-refresh-and-manual-r. Ready for push and PR.

eb9c8c12-df6b-42f5-861f-67e2373c4287.author: maxine--tui-startup-sync
eb9c8c12-df6b-42f5-861f-67e2373c4287.content: Decision: Background thread only does SyncManager::fetch() (network I/O). Hydration (hydrate_to_sqlite) runs on main thread to avoid SQLite threading issues. SyncResult struct carries cache_path and optional error across mpsc channel. PERIODIC_SYNC_INTERVAL = 30s defined as module-level constant for easy tuning.

3f440d5c-4ab1-46ec-afae-7a52d5773c50.title: Auto-repair stale user.signingkey in hub-cache config.worktree after agent worktree deletion (#565)

23c8f3be-41ef-41d4-b3aa-60dfe23d3a1f.author: anon-ab6221f8
23c8f3be-41ef-41d4-b3aa-60dfe23d3a1f.content: Implementation done. Added SyncManager::repair_stale_signingkey() in sync/trust.rs (reads user.signingkey from cache_dir, skips literal key material, falls back to driver signing via existing fallback_to_driver_signing when the path is missing). Wired best-effort call into git_commit_in_cache so every commit self-heals. Extracted tilde-expansion into a module-private helper reused by both code paths. Five new unit tests: missing-path repaired, valid-path noop, literal-key skipped, cache-missing noop, no-worktree-override noop. 1702 lib tests pass. Ready for push + PR.

e1ac73d3-7490-4515-9be1-5736a09e08b7.author: anon-ab6221f8
e1ac73d3-7490-4515-9be1-5736a09e08b7.content: Plan: self-heal at the commit site (issue #565's option 3). Add SyncManager::repair_stale_signingkey that reads user.signingkey from the cache worktree, expands tilde, and if the value is a path that doesn't exist, delegates to the existing fallback_to_driver_signing to rewrite config.worktree (driver key if present, disable signing otherwise). Wire into git_commit_in_cache as a best-effort preamble so every commit self-heals. Factor tilde-expansion into a shared helper so both sites agree. Skip validation when signingkey value looks like literal key material ('ssh-...', '-----BEGIN') rather than a path. Tests: stale path gets repaired; valid path no-op; literal-key skipped; missing signingkey no-op.

03e1c7c8-a43a-4366-bd26-e224846a1816.content: Implementation complete across 4 logical commits on feat/361-issue-scheduling-fields: (1) data model + DB schema v16->v17 migration; (2) SharedWriter create_issue/update_issue threading + FieldUpdate<T> enum; (3) CLI flags on Create/Quick/Update + dispatch + commands/create.rs, update.rs, show.rs, next.rs; (4) 24 new behavioural tests covering AC-1, AC-7, AC-8, AC-9, AC-11, AC-16, AC-20 + REQ-11 and the show/parse/serde roundtrips. de5de893-ccdb-4093-9051-246788c72604.author: anon-ab6221f8
de5de893-ccdb-4093-9051-246788c72604.content: Implementing GH #361 per the design-doc revision in the issue's second comment (Q1 resolved: direct file mutation, no SchedulingUpdated event). Schema is actually at v16 so migration is 16->17 (original spec said 15->16). Approach: single branch with 4 logical commits — data model+DB, hydration+shared_writer, CLI+commands, tests. Using FieldUpdate<T>{Unchanged|Set|Clear} enum in update_issue rather than Option<Option<T>> for clarity. Using ripsed to bulk-add 'scheduled_at: None, due_at: None' to the ~91 Issue struct literals in tests, anchored on the preceding 'closed_at:' line.

70be4eb9-d3a7-4e6b-a552-e48d396f39ed.author: anon-ab6221f8
70be4eb9-d3a7-4e6b-a552-e48d396f39ed.content:
## What

Implement automatic Sonnet -> Opus escalation. When a dispatch fails on Sonnet (attempt 1), it becomes eligible for retry with Opus (attempt 2) after a configurable cooldown.

### How it works
The escalation logic lives in SeenSet.evaluate() (#653) and the dispatch engine (#654). This issue wires them together end-to-end.

### Flow
1. Signal arrives, SeenSet says New -> dispatch with Sonnet (attempt_number=1, model_used='claude-sonnet-4-6')
2. Agent fails (outcome='failure' or 'timeout')
3. Next sentinel cycle: same signal appears again from source
4. SeenSet.evaluate() sees: outcome='failure', attempt_number=1, cooldown elapsed -> returns Escalate
5. Triage engine receives Escalate decision -> builds dispatch with Opus (attempt_number=2, model_used='claude-opus-4-6')
6. Timeout gets 1.5x multiplier: 30min * 1.5 = 45min for Opus attempt
7. If Opus also fails -> outcome='exhausted', never retried again

### Config keys
```json
"escalation": {
    "enabled": true,
    "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
    "cooldown_minutes": 30,
    "max_attempts": 2,
    "timeout_multiplier": 1.5
}
```

### What 'failure' means per dispatch type
- replicate: no failing test produced, or test doesn't compile, or agent timed out
- fix (V1): tests don't pass after fix, or agent couldn't identify root cause

### Structured template for exhausted signals
When both attempts fail, the GH comment should note both:
```
## Sentinel: Reproduction Report
| Status | Could not reproduce (2 attempts exhausted) |
| Attempt 1 | Sonnet — 28m — test compiled but passed (bug not triggered) |
| Attempt 2 | Opus — 41m — identified flaky timing dependency, could not isolate |
```

### Dependencies
- #653 (dedup — SignalDecision::Escalate path)
- #654 (dispatch — model/timeout selection based on decision)
- #655 (collection — exhausted outcome handling)
- #658 (config — escalation settings)

3e524317-f172-4f0a-837e-01ff2642a43d.author: maxine--basel
3e524317-f172-4f0a-837e-01ff2642a43d.content: Render flow now: (1) generate SVGs into docs_src/, (2) quarto renders to .docs_build/ temp dir via QUARTO_PROJECT_OUTPUT_DIR override, (3) collision check scans temp output against docs/ for files with no docs_src/ source (protects api.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ELI5.md), (4) rsync with --filter='protect *.md' copies to docs/ preserving manual files, (5) --force flag overrides collisions, (6) verify step gates output. Added .docs_build/ to .gitignore and scripts/** to docs.yml triggers.

7e55ed80-a85f-42c6-bff3-d7dc8c111e5d.author: maxine--basel
7e55ed80-a85f-42c6-bff3-d7dc8c111e5d.content: Changing render flow: quarto renders to a temp dir, then we diff against docs/ to catch collisions with manually-maintained .md files (api.md, ARCHITECTURE.md, ELI5.md), warn on collisions, and copy with --force flag to override. This protects the GH-browsable docs that coexist in docs/.

78c821b9-3484-41e1-9363-7fe5aa10f235.author: anon-ab6221f8
78c821b9-3484-41e1-9363-7fe5aa10f235.content:
## What

Add sentinel_runs and sentinel_dispatches tables to the SQLite database. Increment SCHEMA_VERSION from 15 to 16.

### Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sentinel_runs (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    run_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    completed_at TEXT,
    mode TEXT NOT NULL,               -- 'oneshot' | 'watch'
    signals_found INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    dispatched INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    collected INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    triaged INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    skipped INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    deferred INTEGER DEFAULT 0
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS sentinel_dispatches (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    run_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    signal_ref TEXT NOT NULL,          -- 'GH#499:replicate', 'GH#499:fix'
    signal_title TEXT NOT NULL,
    source TEXT NOT NULL,
    disposition TEXT NOT NULL,         -- 'dispatch' | 'triage' | 'skip' | 'defer'
    agent_id TEXT,
    crosslink_issue_id INTEGER,
    gh_issue_number INTEGER,
    label TEXT NOT NULL,              -- 'agent-todo: replicate', 'agent-todo: fix'
    attempt_number INTEGER DEFAULT 1, -- 1 = Sonnet, 2 = Opus escalation
    model_used TEXT,
    outcome TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',   -- 'pending' | 'success' | 'failure' | 'timeout' | 'orphaned' | 'exhausted'
    outcome_detail TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    completed_at TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (crosslink_issue_id) REFERENCES issues(id)
);
```

### Indexes
- signal_ref (fast SeenSet loading)
- outcome (fast in-flight count)
- run_id (join to sentinel_runs)
- (gh_issue_number, label) composite (authoritative dedup check)

### Where to edit
- `crosslink/src/db/core.rs`: Increment SCHEMA_VERSION from 15 to 16, add migration in `migrate()`
- Add helper methods: `insert_sentinel_run()`, `insert_sentinel_dispatch()`, `update_dispatch_outcome()`, `get_pending_dispatches()`, `get_dispatch_by_signal_ref()`
- These go in a new file `crosslink/src/db/sentinel.rs` to keep db/core.rs from growing further

### Why attempt_number and model_used columns
The escalation design (Sonnet first, Opus on retry) needs to know what was already tried. These columns let SeenSet determine whether to escalate or give up without additional table joins.

### Testing
- Migration test: open a v15 DB, run migrate(), verify tables exist
- Insert/query round-trip for both tables
- Verify FK constraint on crosslink_issue_id
- Verify existing data is unaffected by migration

5bd9d999-936a-471b-af18-631a7d1b4f56.title: V0: sentinel config in hook-config.json + config registry

77385938-0c10-413e-a7a4-a906c4ddc4b8.author: anon-ab6221f8
77385938-0c10-413e-a7a4-a906c4ddc4b8.content:
## What

Add sentinel configuration to hook-config.json and register all keys in config_registry.rs.

### Config structure
New top-level key in .crosslink/hook-config.json:
```json
{
    "sentinel": {
        "enabled": true,
        "interval_minutes": 10,
        "max_concurrent_agents": 3,
        "sources": {
            "github_labels": {
                "enabled": true,
                "labels": ["agent-todo: replicate", "agent-todo: fix"]
            }
        },
        "default_agent": {
            "model": "claude-sonnet-4-6",
            "timeout_minutes": 30,
            "verify": "local"
        },
        "escalation": {
            "enabled": true,
            "model": "claude-opus-4-6",
            "cooldown_minutes": 30,
            "max_attempts": 2,
            "timeout_multiplier": 1.5
        }
    }
}
```

### SentinelConfig struct
In config.rs: parse the above into a typed struct with serde. All fields have defaults so missing keys don't break existing installs.

### Config registry
Add entries to the static array in commands/config_registry.rs (~line 60):
- sentinel.enabled (Bool, group: Sentinel, hot-swappable: true)
- sentinel.interval_minutes (Integer, range 1-1440)
- sentinel.max_concurrent_agents (Integer, range 1-10)
- sentinel.sources.github_labels.enabled (Bool)
- sentinel.sources.github_labels.labels (StringArray)
- sentinel.default_agent.model (String)
- sentinel.default_agent.timeout_minutes (Integer, range 5-480)
- sentinel.default_agent.verify (Enum: local, ci, thorough)
- sentinel.escalation.enabled (Bool)
- sentinel.escalation.model (String)
- sentinel.escalation.cooldown_minutes (Integer, range 5-1440)
- sentinel.escalation.max_attempts (Integer, range 1-5)
- sentinel.escalation.timeout_multiplier — store as integer (150 = 1.5x) to avoid float config type

### Default config template
Update resources/crosslink/hook-config.json with the sentinel block so `crosslink init` creates it for new projects.

### Dependencies
- #650 (module skeleton — config.rs lives there)

Added Bash(tmux *) and Bash(git worktree *) as the two permission patterns — broad enough to cover all subcommands without needing individual entries.

Create a write_settings_json_merged() function (similar to existing write_mcp_json_merged()) that merges allowedTools arrays on re-init rather than overwriting. Key files: crosslink/resources/claude/settings.json (template), crosslink/src/commands/init.rs (merge logic + tests). The template gets Bash(tmux *) and Bash(git worktree *) entries. The merge function reads existing settings.json, unions the allowedTools arrays, and overwrites hooks (which are crosslink-managed).

b459137f-640a-4396-a82b-66eebce0c38d.author: dashboard-bootstrap
b459137f-640a-4396-a82b-66eebce0c38d.content:
Fix #602: 4-part repair safety net for integrity hydration --repair.

(1) Snapshot: every --repair run writes .crosslink/integrity/hydration-backup-<utc-ts>.sqlite via VACUUM INTO before touching shared data. Self-contained, WAL-safe, no new feature flags.

(2) Content drift detection: new commands::integrity_drift::detect() builds a JSON-derived view in a temp SQLite, ATTACHes to the main connection, and SQL-diffs every shared table (labels, deps, relations, comments, milestone_issues, time_entries) plus sqlite-only issues. Catches divergence the count check missed.

(3) Re-emit (SQLite -> JSON): re_emit() calls SharedWriter.{add_label, add_blocker, add_relation, set_milestone_on_issues} for each detected SQLite-only row, writing them back through the git event log. Comments/time_entries cannot be re-emitted (no JSON representation / no writer API).

(4) --accept-data-loss flag: when drift includes unrecoverable rows (comments, time_entries) or a writer isn't available, --repair refuses without the flag and points the user at the snapshot.

Tests: 2875 pass (+9). Clippy clean -D warnings. New files: db/snapshot.rs, commands/integrity_drift.rs.

b2cd850a-cebc-462d-b5a8-fc06bddc0102.author: anon-ab6221f8
b2cd850a-cebc-462d-b5a8-fc06bddc0102.content:
## What

Implement result collection — the other half of the sentinel cycle. Polls completed agents, reads their findings, posts structured results to GitHub, and updates dispatch records.

### collect_completed()
Runs every sentinel cycle (after dispatch phase in oneshot, every cycle in watch mode).

1. Query: `SELECT * FROM sentinel_dispatches WHERE outcome = 'pending'`
2. For each pending dispatch:
   a. Check if worktree still exists (`.worktrees/<agent_id>`)
      - If not: mark outcome = 'orphaned', skip GH comment, continue
   b. Check for `.kickoff-status` sentinel file in worktree
      - If absent: agent still running, skip
      - If present: read content
   c. Determine outcome: 'DONE' prefix -> 'success', anything else -> 'failure'
   d. Read agent's crosslink comments (filtered by kind = 'observation' or 'resolution')
   e. Check for .kickoff-report.json (may have structured data)
   f. Build structured GH comment from template
   g. Check GH comment dedup (Layer 4 — look for 'sentinel #<dispatch-id>' marker)
   h. Post to GH issue via `gh issue comment <N> --body "<template>"`
   i. Update dispatch: outcome, outcome_detail, completed_at

### Structured templates

**Reproduction template** sections:
| Field | Source |
|-------|--------|
| Status | Parse from agent comments or .kickoff-status |
| Agent | dispatch.agent_id |
| Model | dispatch.model_used |
| Attempt | dispatch.attempt_number |
| Duration | now - dispatch.created_at |
| Test file | grep agent comments for test file paths |
| Findings | concatenate observation/resolution comments |
| Test output | read from .kickoff-report.json or last comment |
| Next steps | static: 'Review test' + 'Label agent-todo: fix' |

The template is compiled in Rust as a format string, NOT generated by the agent. The agent produces raw findings; sentinel structures them.

### GH comment posting
Use: `gh issue comment <number> --body "<markdown>"`
The body must include the marker: `sentinel #<dispatch-id>` for dedup (Layer 4).
Truncate test output to 50 lines to avoid GitHub's comment size limit.

### Orphaned dispatch handling
If a worktree was cleaned up (via `kickoff cleanup`) before sentinel collected results:
- Mark outcome = 'orphaned'
- Do NOT post a GH comment (we have nothing to report)
- Log a warning

### Dependencies
- #651 (schema — update methods)
- #653 (dedup — Layer 4 GH comment check)
- #654 (dispatch — pending dispatches to collect from)

cd6e8cf6-0ff4-4673-bd1f-38d6687f22f9.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
cd6e8cf6-0ff4-4673-bd1f-38d6687f22f9.content: Decision: For release-builds.yml, upload-artifact@v4 works with contents: read — artifact upload uses the implicit ACTIONS_RUNTIME_TOKEN, not the GITHUB_TOKEN permissions. For publish.yml, contents: read is sufficient since the crates.io publish uses CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN (a secret), not GitHub permissions.

ce108b2c-5760-4065-a6e9-5baa6121ec08.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci
ce108b2c-5760-4065-a6e9-5baa6121ec08.content: Decision: T2+T3 combined — removed continue-on-error from all fuzz steps so crashes fail CI. Increased regular CI fuzz from 30s to 60s per target, and now running all 6 existing targets (was only 3). Created fuzz-nightly.yml with schedule cron (3 AM UTC daily) running all targets including new ones for 300s each via matrix strategy. Nightly uses fail-fast: false so one crashing target doesn't prevent others from running.

de337fd4-2855-4a4b-82af-030d75418a4f.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fuzz-ci

6a191e74-1920-43b2-b772-41b969f660c1.title: Signing: human key always signs hub commits; agent keys only for identity

268af4ba-12c4-4eb1-bf2b-55a1fe839628.author: dashboard-bootstrap
268af4ba-12c4-4eb1-bf2b-55a1fe839628.content:
Shipped in d3e7772c. configure_signing is now role-aware:

• AgentRole::Driver workspaces → hub-cache user.signingkey = driver's main-repo user.signingkey (the human's GitHub-registered key). Commits verify end-to-end on GitHub.
• AgentRole::Agent worktrees (kickoff/swarm subagents) → hub-cache signs with agent's own SSH key. Distinct attribution; local verification via allowed_signers.

Other changes:
• crosslink agent init gained --role <driver|agent> (default 'agent' preserves kickoff behavior; dashboard retrofit passes --role driver explicitly).
• configure_signing now runs unconditionally after key-gen (previously nested inside the Ok(keypair) arm, so --force on existing-key workspaces was a no-op).
• Fixed /home/doll/crosslink/.crosslink/agent.json.role from 'agent' to 'driver'; re-ran agent init; hub-cache user.signingkey flipped from dashboard-bootstrap key to /home/doll/.ssh/id_ecdsa_signing (the driver's ED25519 key — filename is misleading, contents are ED25519 with fingerprint 6GbEO9g...).
• New sync commit 9834eb7a verifies as 'Good git signature for driver@crosslink'.

Past hub commits on origin signed with the dashboard-bootstrap key will remain unverified on GitHub unless the user adds that pub key as a signing key there. Flagged to the user.

6b623c72-9daf-4641-8c4b-02be7c38f881.title: Fix GH#587: CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR prefix lands after timeout, breaks every kickoff launch

1ee8ad9d-ba58-4668-92b7-59c5d732f69c.author: dashboard-bootstrap
1ee8ad9d-ba58-4668-92b7-59c5d732f69c.content: CI failure follow-up: the 3 new integration tests assumed 'timeout' is on PATH. The CI runner that failed had neither 'timeout' nor 'gtimeout' (likely macOS without 'brew install coreutils', or a stripped image). Added resolve_test_timeout_cmd() helper that probes for either binary via --version and returns Some(name) when usable, None otherwise. Each test now early-returns with an eprintln skip message when no usable timeout binary is available — passes cleanly instead of false-failing. Also replaced the sandbox passthrough stub from 'env --' (GNU-specific, BSD env may reject) to a tempdir-written shell script ('#!/bin/sh\nexec "$\@"') so the sandbox test is portable across macOS too. cargo fmt clean, clippy clean (resolved a manual-find-implementation warning along the way), all 11 build_agent_command tests pass locally with timeout present.

4c507211-474d-4d6a-8da4-fa2c4d2490eb.author: dashboard-bootstrap
4c507211-474d-4d6a-8da4-fa2c4d2490eb.content: Applied Option A from GH#587: fold the CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR=val assignment into env(1)'s argv between '-u CLAUDECODE' and 'claude', rather than emitting it as a shell prefix. The 0.8.0 build placed the assignment AFTER 'timeout' at column zero, where shell grammar treats VAR=val as a literal positional arg — timeout then tried to exec it as a binary path (ENOENT). Folding into env's argv is robust regardless of what wraps the line (timeout today, nice/chrt/bwrap tomorrow). Three new integration tests #[cfg(unix)] actually shell-execute the constructed command with a stub claude that prints CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR — these are the 'real shell parses this' tests the issue called out as the missing regression-prevention shape: env-var actually reaches claude (without sandbox), through a sandbox wrapper, and omitted-env-var does not break launch. Updated docstring + the two existing string-shape tests for the new shape. Full suite: 2820 passed (2817 baseline + 3 new). cargo fmt clean, clippy clean.

5b46eeed-e268-4e2d-848c-7469c7d4118a.content: The bug: configure_signing() in trust.rs returns early (line 34-36) when agent key is missing, but doesn't clean up the stale signing config already set in the hub cache worktree. 5c8dc19a-23cb-40bf-a2b2-88d30366fce8.author: anon-ab6221f8
5c8dc19a-23cb-40bf-a2b2-88d30366fce8.content: Implemented fallback_to_driver_signing() in trust.rs and disable_git_signing() in signing.rs. When configure_signing detects the agent key is gone, it reads the driver user.signingkey from the main repo git config and reconfigures the hub cache to use it. If no driver key exists, signing is disabled gracefully so commits proceed unsigned rather than failing.

ff170a17-30c2-4736-9bf8-00842d18b960.author: anon-ab6221f8
ff170a17-30c2-4736-9bf8-00842d18b960.content: Root cause: topo_sort_issues and dedup_issue_files don't impose a stable ordering on issues without display_id. Iteration order comes from filesystem which is non-deterministic. When next_local_id always starts at -1, a re-hydration that reads files in a different order swaps local ID assignments. Fix: add stable tiebreaker (created_at then uuid) to topo_sort_issues so roots always appear in the same order across hydrations.

71387690-a5f5-4a76-889c-5d8b16410c54.title: Fix hub cache recovery loop caused by tracked .hub-write-lock file

7740cddf-01b4-4ff9-b054-3d09f474bcf2.author: maxine--basel
7740cddf-01b4-4ff9-b054-3d09f474bcf2.content:
Workaround: manually reset hub cache to origin and add .hub-write-lock to .gitignore on the hub branch:
  cd .crosslink/.hub-cache
  git reset --hard origin/crosslink/hub
  echo .hub-write-lock >> .gitignore
  git rm --cached .hub-write-lock
  git add .gitignore
  git commit -m 'fix: exclude .hub-write-lock from tracking'
  git push origin crosslink/hub

Proper fix should also update init_cache() or upgrade_to_v2() to ensure .hub-write-lock is gitignored when creating new hub branches.

a608d5f9-26f7-4e8f-abb0-b40b835ea07a.author: dashboard-bootstrap
a608d5f9-26f7-4e8f-abb0-b40b835ea07a.content: Hub cache recovery loop — not reproduced since 2026-03-27; closing as stale, reopen if it resurfaces

c8f8a795-024f-4119-a1b8-0767f35f7782.author: maxine--basel
c8f8a795-024f-4119-a1b8-0767f35f7782.content:
Root cause: .hub-write-lock is a runtime PID lock file (created/deleted by HubWriteLock RAII guard in sync/cache.rs) but it's tracked in the crosslink/hub git branch. Every sync cycle: (1) acquire_lock writes PID to file, (2) drop deletes it, (3) next clean_dirty_state() sees dirty state, (4) commits 'sync: auto-stage dirty hub state (recovery)', (5) can't push because of divergence, (6) repeat. This created 274 recovery commits in one crash cycle.

Fix: add .hub-write-lock to .gitignore on the hub branch and git rm --cached it. The lock file works fine untracked — it's OS-level file locking with PID liveness checks.

Affected code: crosslink/src/sync/cache.rs lines 41-52 (try_create_lock), lines 25-37 (Drop impl), lines 460-504 (clean_dirty_state).

5423974e-79f7-410b-8189-3f81cc79b58a.content: Creating a GitHub issue to track automation of 13 smoke/manual tests identified in audit of PRs #175-#225. Tests will be tiered to match CI pipeline: fast unit/integration tests for feature branches, heavier git-fixture tests for develop/release gates.

781606a3-280a-4849-8c59-a8f289805ae0.title: Fix TUI adversarial review findings (C1, C2, H1-H4, M1-M7)

0fd5ac11-ab79-4406-a298-6d1a5aa8487c.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--tui-dashboard
0fd5ac11-ab79-4406-a298-6d1a5aa8487c.content:
Fixed ADR findings:

CRITICAL: C1 (RAII terminal guard), C2 (UTF-8 safe truncation)
HIGH: H2 (lazy DB open), H3 (event_scroll usize), H4 (panic hook restore)
MEDIUM: M3 (clipboard flash feedback), M4 (detail Esc to prev view), M5 (tab click char width), M6 (flash only on click/scroll), M7 (tree depth limit)
LOW: L4 (unused field), L7 (future timestamp), L8 (y:Copy hints)

Deferred: H1 (async I/O), M1 (scroll clamping), M2 (q from detail), L1-L3/L5-L6 (minor consistency)

123 tests pass, clippy clean, Cargo.lock regenerated.

17ef81e9-41f1-43a9-ba78-aac5a1a3b49e.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--tui-dashboard
17ef81e9-41f1-43a9-ba78-aac5a1a3b49e.content:
Adversarial review of TUI (all 5 phases). Consolidated findings:

CRITICAL (2):
- C1: Terminal not restored on error paths — if App::new(), terminal.draw(), or event::read() returns Err, cleanup is skipped. Terminal left in raw mode. Fix: RAII guard struct.
- C2: truncate_str/truncate panics on multi-byte UTF-8 — uses byte indexing `&s[..n]`. Any non-ASCII agent ID or branch name crashes TUI. Fix: use char_indices().

HIGH (4):
- H1: Blocking network I/O (sync.fetch()) freezes TUI on slow/offline networks (agents_tab, knowledge_tab).
- H2: IssuesTab opens a new DB connection on every keystroke, even arrow navigation.
- H3: event_scroll u16 truncation silently wraps for >65K events in config_tab.
- H4: Panic hook never restored after run() returns; calling run() twice chains hooks.

MEDIUM (7):
- M1: Unbounded scroll in all detail views — can scroll past content into blank space.
- M2: 'q' quits from issue detail view with no confirmation.
- M3: Clipboard copy (y key) gives no feedback — result silently ignored.
- M4: Detail Esc always returns to List, not Tree view.
- M5: Mouse tab-bar click uses byte len() not display width; border clicks trigger switch.
- M6: Flash message cleared on any mouse event including hover.
- M7: build_tree_recursive has no depth limit or cycle detection.

LOW (8):
- L1: Inconsistent on_enter refresh across tabs.
- L2: Status bar r:Refresh hint without global binding.
- L3: Unused content_area field.
- L4: Help overlay has no scroll support.
- L5: Tag filter cycles by index not name (loses position on refresh).
- L6: Negative time display for future timestamps.
- L7: Missing y:Copy hints in detail view footers.

NIT (5): Duplicate truncate/format_relative_time helpers, SyncManager created twice per config refresh, dead PlaceholderTab, hardcoded tab count range.

Recommended fix priority: C1 (terminal guard), C2 (UTF-8 safe truncation), H4 (panic hook restore), M3 (clipboard feedback).

7f2d4cbe-d193-48e5-91b3-1152736b514e.title: V1: agent-todo:fix dispatch rule — VerifyLevel::Ci + draft PR

50133d86-75bd-4f69-8d49-a751974ca267.author: anon-ab6221f8
50133d86-75bd-4f69-8d49-a751974ca267.content:
## What

Add the `agent-todo: fix` dispatch rule. Fix agents auto-dispatch with VerifyLevel::Ci — they push a branch and open a draft PR for human review (Q2 decision).

### Triage rule
Add to the match in dispatch.rs:
```rust
"agent-todo: fix" => Disposition::Dispatch {
    description: format!("Fix bug from GH#{}: {}", signal.reference, signal.title),
    scope: AgentScope {
        allowed_paths: vec!["src/".into(), "tests/".into()],
        verify: VerifyLevel::Ci,
        timeout: Duration::from_secs(3600), // 1 hour
        model,
    },
    attempt,
},
```

### Fix agent prompt
Scoped prompt for fix agents:
1. Read the issue
2. Explore the codebase to find root cause
3. Write a failing test
4. Implement the fix
5. Run full test suite
6. Record findings via crosslink comments
7. Push branch and open draft PR

PR title format: `fix: <issue title> (sentinel #<dispatch-id>)`

### VerifyLevel::Ci requirements
Unlike Local, Ci means:
- Agent is allowed to push (need to configure git push in allowed_tools)
- Agent creates a draft PR via `gh pr create --draft`
- Agent needs `gh auth` credentials in the worktree

### Credential propagation
The agent worktree needs to be able to push. Options:
- Copy the user's git credential helper config to the worktree's local .gitconfig
- Set GH_TOKEN environment variable at launch time (read from `gh auth token`)
- Best approach: at launch time, run `gh auth token` to get the token, pass it as GH_TOKEN env var to the tmux session. This avoids touching git config.

### Fix result template
New template variant for fix dispatches:
- PR link instead of test file
- Files changed count + lines diff
- Changes summary section

### Dependencies
- #654 (dispatch engine — add rule to triage)
- #655 (collection — fix template for GH comment)
- #658 (config — "agent-todo: fix" in labels list)
- All V0 issues (fix builds on the infrastructure)

9166a319-a7f9-48ca-bcde-c084e7631af9.author: anon-ab6221f8
9166a319-a7f9-48ca-bcde-c084e7631af9.content:
Shipped in 8c2707fb. Three layered bugs fixed + clone-by-URL feature added:

1. Close-issue not vanishing — root cause was THREE overlapping bugs: resolve_hub_root preferring stale nested worktree, ensure_hub_cache_worktree wiping in-flight CLI writes via reset --hard on dirty trees, and crosslink issue close not committing to hub (writes to worktree but queues commit for later sync). Fixed by swapping worktree preference, skipping reset on dirty worktrees, and running 'crosslink sync -q' after every run_cli write action.

2. Init failing — crosslink init writes placeholder .crosslink/agent.json then agent init errors 'already configured'. Added --force to BOTH init and agent init in retrofit path.

3. Clone-by-URL — new POST /api/v1/dashboard/clone endpoint + new Settings → GitHub form (above the org browser, no PAT required). Accepts URL + optional slug override + optional init toggle + agent id. Clones to ~/crosslink-tracked/<owner>/<repo>, tracks, optionally inits.

Verified end-to-end: #170 and #171 now show status=closed in API; atdata went from not_initialized to ready after dashboard retrofit; rust-lang/mdBook cloned via new endpoint landed in ~/crosslink-tracked/rust-lang/mdBook. Full frontend suite 101/101.

270814c1-04cf-46e1-8599-9e7160f233ea.author: anon-ab6221f8
270814c1-04cf-46e1-8599-9e7160f233ea.content:
## What

Implement `crosslink sentinel watch` — persistent daemon mode. Separate process from the hydration daemon (Q1 decision).

### Process model
- PID file: .crosslink/sentinel.pid
- Log file: .crosslink/sentinel.log
- Signal handlers: SIGTERM, SIGINT (via signal_hook, same as daemon.rs)
- Stdin-closure zombie prevention (same pattern as daemon.rs:151-175)

### Implementation
Follow the daemon.rs pattern exactly for process management:
- `watch()`: spawns self with `sentinel run-daemon --dir <path>` (hidden subcommand)
- `run_watch_loop()`: the actual loop
- `stop()`: reads PID file, kills process
- `status()`: reads PID file, checks if alive, queries last run from DB

### Watch loop
```
loop {
    if should_exit -> break
    run_oneshot_cycle()  // reuse engine from #654
    collect_completed()  // reuse from #655
    sleep(interval * backoff_multiplier)
    if should_exit -> break
}
```

### Backoff
- Success: reset multiplier to 1
- Failure: double multiplier (cap at 8x)
- So: 10min -> 20min -> 40min -> 80min max between retries on repeated failures

### Status output
```
Sentinel daemon running (PID 12345)
  Interval:    10 minutes (next poll in 3m 42s)
  In-flight:   2 / 3 agents
  Last poll:   2026-04-10 14:30:00 (3 signals, 2 dispatched)
  Uptime:      2h 15m
```

### Dependencies
- #650 (module skeleton)
- #654 (dispatch engine — run_oneshot_cycle)
- #655 (collection — collect_completed)
- #658 (config — interval, enabled flag)

506d3cd8-ef28-4588-817d-6a631092d08f.author: dashboard-bootstrap
506d3cd8-ef28-4588-817d-6a631092d08f.content: Committed on feature/767-hub-visible-branches. Namespace flipped to refs/heads/crosslink/*, browse tree (issues + README + milestones) on the checkpoint branch with incremental updates and deterministic content, migrate hub-branches rename command. Director-verified gates: 1767 lib + 2776 bin + 194 integration, fmt, both clippy profiles. Heartbeat visibility restored as a side effect: heartbeat commits appear on agent branches in the web UI. Awaiting push; rollout = migrate hub-branches on dollspace then basel post-merge.

6c6f22dc-ab57-4b2a-9ad7-beb26cc15ae0.author: dashboard-bootstrap
6c6f22dc-ab57-4b2a-9ad7-beb26cc15ae0.content: Design (user requirement: hub MUST be visible on browsable branches -- corrects the OQ-1 hidden-refs decision): (1) NAMESPACE FLIP: refs/crosslink/agents/<id> -> refs/heads/crosslink/agents/<id> (branch crosslink/agents/<id>), checkpoint -> crosslink/checkpoint, meta -> crosslink/meta. CI verified safe: workflows trigger only on develop + feature/**. Enumeration/detection must key on the checkpoint branch specifically and never read crosslink/hub (frozen v2) or crosslink/hub-v3-host (worktree host) as hub state. (2) BROWSABLE STATE: compact_v3 additionally maintains a materialized browse tree on the checkpoint branch -- issues/<uuid>.json rendered from CompactIssue with comments/time entries inline, README.md explaining the branch -- updated incrementally via outcome.changed_issues + tombstone deletions through write_tree_with (NOT full-tree rewrites per compact; 759 issues would make per-mutation compaction heavy). Deterministic content so the force-with-lease race semantics are unchanged. (3) MIGRATION: crosslink migrate hub-branches -- idempotent one-shot per machine: recreate each old refs/crosslink/* at the same SHA under refs/heads/crosslink/*, push new, delete old remote + local. Two deployed machines (dollspace + basel) each run it once; this machine first. (4) Fetch moves to standard remote-tracking refs (or minimal-diff explicit refspec -- implementer decides, reports). Writer-authoritative adoption rules unchanged.

712cc416-ae28-4730-9a06-f406e59c710c.author: dashboard-bootstrap
712cc416-ae28-4730-9a06-f406e59c710c.content: ROLLED OUT on dollspace: migrate hub-branches renamed all 3 refs to visible branches (old namespace empty both sides), browse tree materialized on crosslink/checkpoint (README + issues/ + meta/ + state.json). Remaining: run migrate hub-branches once on basel after updating its binary. This comment itself is the first write through the renamed branch namespace.

72dc82c1-1b13-481c-8343-4817b3b8e0af.author: dashboard-bootstrap

FETCH-TRACKING CHOICE: kept the explicit refs/crosslink-remote/* tracking namespace (option b), updating only the fetch SOURCE patterns to refs/heads/crosslink/*. Smallest correct diff: the adoption logic (list_remote_agent_tips, adopt_checkpoint_by_watermark, remote_checkpoint_sha, shared_writer checkpoint-lease baseline) all read refs/crosslink-remote/* unchanged; option (a) refs/remotes/<remote>/crosslink/* would have threaded the remote name into every tracking-ref read and mixed hub state into the user branch remote-tracking namespace. Namespace flip: AGENT_REF_PREFIX/CHECKPOINT_REF/META_REF -> refs/heads/crosslink/*. Detection keys on exact checkpoint+meta branches; crosslink/hub (v2) and crosslink/hub-v3-host excluded by is_v3_hub_ref. Browse tree: compact_v3 now writes issues/<uuid>.json (comments+time inline), meta/milestones.json, README.md incrementally via changed_issues + tombstone deletes; full tree only when README absent. New command: crosslink migrate hub-branches. Gates green: fmt, clippy --all-targets -D warnings, unwrap/expect clean. Tests: lib 1767, bin 2776, cli_integration 194 — all pass.

1659a527-dda7-4457-94c1-234a44f12df0.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--accept-both-conflict
1659a527-dda7-4457-94c1-234a44f12df0.content:
Plan: Add accept-both conflict resolution to knowledge branch sync/push flow.

Key changes:
1. Add conflict detection (has_conflict_markers) and resolution (resolve_accept_both) functions to knowledge.rs
2. Add SyncOutcome struct to carry resolved conflict info back to callers
3. Add handle_rebase_conflict() method to KnowledgeManager that aborts rebase, falls back to merge, resolves conflicts with accept-both
4. Modify sync() to return Result<SyncOutcome> - catches rebase conflicts and uses accept-both fallback
5. Modify push() to return Result<SyncOutcome> - catches pull --rebase conflicts and uses accept-both fallback
6. Update commands/knowledge.rs callers to handle SyncOutcome, create crosslink issues for resolved conflicts, and print warnings
7. Add comprehensive unit tests for conflict parsing, resolution, and the full flow

Files to modify:
- crosslink/src/knowledge.rs (core conflict resolution logic + sync/push changes)
- crosslink/src/commands/knowledge.rs (handle SyncOutcome, create issues, print warnings)

8d425132-f180-40eb-a9b9-b6a28ae5a8cf.title: Dashboard: detect + remedy tracked clones without crosslink init / agent config

f93eeb75-8dce-4ff9-ba36-227721d4b9d8.author: anon-ab6221f8
f93eeb75-8dce-4ff9-ba36-227721d4b9d8.content:
Shipped in c9e920c3. Live API confirms detection works — forecast-bio/imaging shows 'agent_missing' (matching the release_lock failure the audit log surfaced), forecast-bio/atdata and 7 other track-all'd clones show 'not_initialized', the 5 properly-set-up repos (crosslink, ferrotorch, OpenClaudia, feral, tabular) show 'ready'.

Two remediation paths both work end-to-end:
• At clone time: Settings → GitHub → tick 'Initialize cloned repos', enter agent id, Track All. Backend runs crosslink init + crosslink agent init in each clone before registering.
• After the fact: /project/<slug> shows an amber InitBanner at the top when not ready with an inline agent-id input + Initialize button that hits POST /w/{slug}/init.

The Alerts page now hides action buttons on non-ready projects and shows a NotReadyNotice pointing to the project detail page's init banner, so users don't click Close/Release/Steal and watch nothing happen like before.

Tests: 5 new write_capability unit tests + 1 new SettingsGithub integration test; full backend projects 23/23, frontend 99/99 (both under ulimit safety cap).

Plan: (1) sibling-preserving tree core in hub_v3.rs (read_tree_entries via ls-tree, write_tree_with supporting one nesting level, refactor append/commit_log_bytes/commit_blob_to_ref/commit_files_to_ref onto it); (2) heartbeat write/read on agent refs (reuse locks::Heartbeat schema); (3) agent requests on refs with writer-ownership layout requests-out/<target>--<ulid>.json and requests-ack/<ulid>.json (one nesting level); (4) compact_v3 in hub_v3.rs (reduce RefHubSource -> checkpoint commit_blob_to_ref + push_ref_with_lease + REQ-11 own-ref prune only after checkpoint push). v2 finding: requests are plain JSON with requested_by fingerprint, NOT git-signed; poll::process_pending trusts local cache because sync already rejected bad-signer commits at fetch; reduce only WARNS on unsigned events. e636e6b9-3c20-46ea-95d8-5da970037b14.author: dashboard-bootstrap
e636e6b9-3c20-46ea-95d8-5da970037b14.content: 754a design (auxiliary-state inventory complete). V3 HOMES BY WRITER OWNERSHIP (single-writer-per-ref invariant): heartbeat.json -> agent's own ref tree root; agent requests -> DRIVER's ref tree under requests-out/<target-agent>/<ulid>.json (driver is the writer; the v2 scheme of writing into the target agent's directory would violate single-writer); acks -> target agent's own ref tree under requests-ack/<ulid>.json; readers scan all agent refs. Locks: pure events (v2 locks.json + locks/ die). Skew warnings: already in CheckpointState. Cross-machine compaction arbitration: the checkpoint ref force-with-lease push (local compaction.lock stays as cheap local lease). CRITICAL INFRA PREREQ: append_event_to_ref currently builds a single-file tree and would DROP sibling files once refs carry heartbeats/acks -- tree building must read existing entries at tip and replace only the target file. Execution: 754a in three passes -- (1) hub_v3 infra: sibling-preserving trees, heartbeats/requests/acks on refs, v3 compact (checkpoint push with lease + own-ref prune per REQ-11); (2) mode routing: HubMode by detect_hub_version, v3 event-only writes with claim-confirm create, fetch refs + reduce + hydrate-from-CheckpointState into SQLite; (3) direct-reader sweep: server/dashboard/TUI paths reading hub files (locks.json, heartbeats) rerouted through the state/heartbeat modules. 754b (delete v2 machinery + hard refusal) ships as a separate PR after this repo migrates for real.

14b7e88a-ced0-416c-a4b6-0fd896f73c49.author: maxine--knowledge-edit
14b7e88a-ced0-416c-a4b6-0fd896f73c49.content: Decision: Used simple line-by-line section parser rather than an external markdown crate. Heading matching supports both '## Architecture' and 'Architecture' query forms. Subsections are included in the parent section range (replaced/appended along with it). Added #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] on edit() rather than introducing a params struct — keeps the change minimal.

2211d9a6-472e-4c66-9032-c9adced5486d.author: maxine--plan-050
2211d9a6-472e-4c66-9032-c9adced5486d.content: Covers REQ-1, AC-1 through AC-3. Needs: section parser (~80 lines), two new clap flags (~30 lines), edit logic (~100 lines), tests (~150 lines). No existing section parsing in knowledge.rs — must be built from scratch. Key risk: mutual exclusivity with existing --append flag requires clap argument group.

5f6996cc-d629-4669-8770-8e7c67bca4eb.author: maxine--knowledge-edit
5f6996cc-d629-4669-8770-8e7c67bca4eb.content: Plan: Add --replace-section and --append-to-section flags to knowledge edit command. Section parser splits body by markdown headings (^#{1,6} ), tracks heading levels. replace_section_content replaces everything between heading and next same-or-higher-level heading. append_to_section_content appends before next heading. Clap argument group enforces mutual exclusivity with --append. Both require --content.

3feb4694-4433-40d1-b53f-c3fe9d9a0d67.author: m1--full-system-adversarial-review
3feb4694-4433-40d1-b53f-c3fe9d9a0d67.content:
Result: Full adversarial review ADR documented. 30 recommendations across 6 categories:

## Fixes Applied (2)
- F1 CRITICAL: Path traversal in knowledge slugs — fixed with safe_page_path() + 6 tests
- F2 HIGH: Unicode panic in truncate — fixed by delegating to crate::utils::truncate

## Security — Signing & Trust (7)
- S1 HIGH: SSH keys generated without passphrase (-N "")
- S2 HIGH: allowed_signers has no cryptographic integrity protection
- S3 HIGH: No key revocation or rotation mechanism
- S4 MEDIUM: verify_content() only checks exit code, doesn't parse output
- S5 MEDIUM: AllowedSigners parsing accepts any string as public key
- S6 MEDIUM: No audit logging of signature verification results
- S7 MEDIUM: Trust modifications don't record approver metadata

## Concurrency & Data Integrity (6)
- C1 HIGH: TOCTOU race in claim_lock() — two agents can claim same lock
- C2 HIGH: Display ID collision under concurrent creation
- C3 HIGH: Daemon silently swallows DB/sync init errors
- C4 HIGH: Daemon has no SIGTERM/SIGINT handler
- C5 MEDIUM: File writes not atomic (std::fs::write is not atomic)
- C6 MEDIUM: Stale lock detection vulnerable to clock skew

## Database (5)
- D1 HIGH: Silent error suppression in schema migrations (let _ = ...)
- D2 MEDIUM: No CHECK constraints on enum columns
- D3 MEDIUM: Transaction rollback errors silently suppressed
- D4 MEDIUM: parse_datetime silently falls back to Utc::now()
- D5 LOW: UUID column nullable with UNIQUE index

## Input Validation (4)
- V1 MEDIUM: No max length on string inputs (titles, descriptions, comments)
- V2 MEDIUM: No enum validation for status/priority/kind
- V3 MEDIUM: JSON import deserialization has no size limits
- V4 LOW: Agent ID allows single-character names

## CI/CD & Testing (5)
- T1 HIGH: Only 6 of 30+ code paths fuzzed
- T2 MEDIUM: Fuzz CI jobs use continue-on-error: true
- T3 MEDIUM: Fuzz durations only 30 seconds
- T4 MEDIUM: No explicit permissions block on CI jobs
- T5 MEDIUM: Cargo deps use broad version ranges

## VS Code Extension (3)
- E1 MEDIUM: Binary installed without checksum verification
- E2 MEDIUM: execSync used instead of execFileSync
- E3 LOW: Daemon output not sanitized for ANSI codes

## Triaged as Non-Issues (5)
- Export path (user already has fs access), symlink attacks (local-only dir), principal verification (ssh-keygen handles it), DB file permissions (standard for CLI), rate limiting (not exposed to untrusted callers)

Full details in ADR.md. Commit 5be0eca contains the two fixes.

40a3a470-2a6b-43ee-95b1-4817f8ebd833.author: m1--full-system-adversarial-review
40a3a470-2a6b-43ee-95b1-4817f8ebd833.content: Observation: /ad skill failed due to 'chainlink' vs 'crosslink' command name mismatch in skill definition. Proceeding with manual structured adversarial review covering: (1) SQL injection in db.rs, (2) identity/signing security, (3) daemon resource leaks, (4) knowledge input validation, (5) fuzz coverage gaps, (6) dependency audit, (7) CI/CD review, (8) general code quality.

4476e6e9-dcdf-4771-8e05-b75f160029ae.author: m1--full-system-adversarial-review
4476e6e9-dcdf-4771-8e05-b75f160029ae.content:
Result: Committed fix for 2 critical issues:
1. CRITICAL — Path traversal in knowledge page slug (knowledge.rs): Added safe_page_path() with character validation + canonicalization defense-in-depth. 6 new tests.
2. HIGH — Unicode panic in truncate (commands/knowledge.rs): Replaced byte-based slicing with crate::utils::truncate which uses .chars().take().

All 1055+ tests pass. Commit: 5be0eca

5c62e885-57ab-469b-a73f-70fb239f01ca.author: m1--full-system-adversarial-review

Will review the entire codebase for security issues, code quality problems, architectural concerns, and potential bugs. Key areas: db.rs (SQL injection), identity.rs (signing security), daemon.rs (resource leaks), knowledge.rs (input validation), fuzz targets (coverage gaps).

5e712065-5805-4445-a27b-d97b61cff0cc.author: m1--full-system-adversarial-review
5e712065-5805-4445-a27b-d97b61cff0cc.content:
Result: Full adversarial review ADR documented. 30 recommendations across 6 categories:

## Fixes Applied (2)
- F1 CRITICAL: Path traversal in knowledge slugs — fixed with safe_page_path() + 6 tests
- F2 HIGH: Unicode panic in truncate — fixed by delegating to crate::utils::truncate

## Security — Signing & Trust (7)
- S1 HIGH: SSH keys generated without passphrase (-N "")
- S2 HIGH: allowed_signers has no cryptographic integrity protection
- S3 HIGH: No key revocation or rotation mechanism
- S4 MEDIUM: verify_content() only checks exit code, doesn't parse output
- S5 MEDIUM: AllowedSigners parsing accepts any string as public key
- S6 MEDIUM: No audit logging of signature verification results
- S7 MEDIUM: Trust modifications don't record approver metadata

## Concurrency & Data Integrity (6)
- C1 HIGH: TOCTOU race in claim_lock() — two agents can claim same lock
- C2 HIGH: Display ID collision under concurrent creation
- C3 HIGH: Daemon silently swallows DB/sync init errors
- C4 HIGH: Daemon has no SIGTERM/SIGINT handler
- C5 MEDIUM: File writes not atomic (std::fs::write is not atomic)
- C6 MEDIUM: Stale lock detection vulnerable to clock skew

## Database (5)
- D1 HIGH: Silent error suppression in schema migrations (let _ = ...)
- D2 MEDIUM: No CHECK constraints on enum columns
- D3 MEDIUM: Transaction rollback errors silently suppressed
- D4 MEDIUM: parse_datetime silently falls back to Utc::now()
- D5 LOW: UUID column nullable with UNIQUE index

## Input Validation (4)
- V1 MEDIUM: No max length on string inputs (titles, descriptions, comments)
- V2 MEDIUM: No enum validation for status/priority/kind
- V3 MEDIUM: JSON import deserialization has no size limits
- V4 LOW: Agent ID allows single-character names

## CI/CD & Testing (5)
- T1 HIGH: Only 6 of 30+ code paths fuzzed
- T2 MEDIUM: Fuzz CI jobs use continue-on-error: true
- T3 MEDIUM: Fuzz durations only 30 seconds
- T4 MEDIUM: No explicit permissions block on CI jobs
- T5 MEDIUM: Cargo deps use broad version ranges

## VS Code Extension (3)
- E1 MEDIUM: Binary installed without checksum verification
- E2 MEDIUM: execSync used instead of execFileSync
- E3 LOW: Daemon output not sanitized for ANSI codes

## Triaged as Non-Issues (5)
- Export path (user already has fs access), symlink attacks (local-only dir), principal verification (ssh-keygen handles it), DB file permissions (standard for CLI), rate limiting (not exposed to untrusted callers)

Full details in ADR.md. Commit 5be0eca contains the two fixes.
