Proceeding with manual structured adversarial review covering: (1) SQL injection in db.rs, (2) identity/signing security, (3) daemon resource leaks, (4) knowledge input validation, (5) fuzz coverage gaps, (6) dependency audit, (7) CI/CD review, (8) general code quality.

9c17c79d-d1f8-4395-9f87-9e4ddd8076ae.author: m1--full-system-adversarial-review
9c17c79d-d1f8-4395-9f87-9e4ddd8076ae.content:
Decision: After triaging all ~60 findings, identified 2 issues that require code fixes (security/correctness bugs):

1. CRITICAL — Knowledge page slug path traversal (knowledge.rs:289,301,312): Slug passed directly to file path without validation. Allows reading/writing/deleting files outside the knowledge cache via '../' sequences.

2. HIGH — Byte-based string truncate panic (commands/knowledge.rs:347): Uses byte slicing (&s[..n]) which panics on multi-byte Unicode. Safe version already exists in utils.rs.

All other findings are either: (a) not real vulnerabilities for a local CLI tool (export path, symlinks — user already has filesystem access), (b) architectural recommendations (key revocation, audit logging), (c) hardening suggestions (file permissions, deserialization limits, CHECK constraints), or (d) incorrect analysis (principal verification is actually handled correctly by ssh-keygen -Y verify). These will be documented as recommendations only.

e7e3b09f-9d77-45ad-9450-53e7f4fc6678.author: anon-ab6221f8
e7e3b09f-9d77-45ad-9450-53e7f4fc6678.content:
Shipped in 09625074. End-to-end verified:

• Hub data: fetch_hub now uses refspec +refs/heads/crosslink/hub:refs/heads/crosslink/hub + ensure_hub_cache_worktree creates .crosslink/.hub-cache/ via git worktree add (first run) or git reset --hard (subsequent). forecast-bio/atdata shows 6 open issues instead of 0, molten shows 325, etc.

• Issue order: api::load_project_detail sorts open-before-closed, then display_id ascending, then uuid for local-only stragglers. Verified deterministic across successive fetches.

• Token persistence: generate_auth_token reads/writes ~/.crosslink/.dashboard-token (0600 perms); first-run produces real 128-bit random (previously bugged — pid<<64 on u128 always left top 64 bits zero, giving the '0000000000…' prefix every token this session had). --rotate-token flag forces regeneration. Restart preserves the URL; open tabs no longer 401.

Also swept 7 pre-existing clippy -D warnings lints (let-else, doc backticks, const fn, Option<Option<>> allow+comment, as_ref().cloned, print_literal) in the same commit since they blocked cargo clippy --lib --bin crosslink -- -D warnings. All 36 dashboard module tests pass under ulimit -v 8388608 safety cap.

0998c919-814d-4b9e-b302-8347be5071f1.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
0998c919-814d-4b9e-b302-8347be5071f1.content: Researching GH #106 (agent key isolation) and #107 (allowed_signers trust model). Will review current implementation, think through design tradeoffs, and write a knowledge page with findings.

5fb49fba-358c-4044-9d1d-8e4846640312.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
5fb49fba-358c-4044-9d1d-8e4846640312.content: Key observation: The two issues are deeply linked. #106 (key isolation) is about preventing agents from impersonating each other at the crypto layer. #107 (trust nomenclature) is about whether the system's naming overpromises on what guarantees are actually provided. Container isolation (PR #150, already in progress) is the natural answer to #106, and it clarifies #107 — when agents are isolated, allowed_signers becomes clearly a 'phonebook' for verifying others' signatures, not an access control list.

65660045-e0bd-442c-9639-a60264de710b.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
65660045-e0bd-442c-9639-a60264de710b.content: Researching GH #106 (agent key isolation) and #107 (allowed_signers trust model). Will review current implementation, think through design tradeoffs, and write a knowledge page with findings.

bd3fc710-8609-42f9-903d-b56bc46b80ba.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
bd3fc710-8609-42f9-903d-b56bc46b80ba.content: Delivered: Knowledge page 'signing-trust-design' written with full analysis of GH #106 (agent key isolation) and #107 (allowed_signers trust model). Key recommendations: (1) Container isolation is the right answer for #106, current permissions adequate for non-containerized, (2) Rename allowed_signers → known_signers for #107, (3) Add valid-after timestamps for lifecycle, (4) Don't build crypto-on-crypto integrity protection.

a0532b9b-1172-4aa5-b963-8a0324718030.title: V1: in-flight agent tracking + concurrent limit enforcement

9cd743e9-f8e3-438b-ac5b-c5ac638fedac.author: anon-ab6221f8
9cd743e9-f8e3-438b-ac5b-c5ac638fedac.content:
## What

Implement concurrent agent limit enforcement and in-flight tracking. Sentinel must not spawn more agents than max_concurrent_agents (default 3).

### In-flight count
Two sources of truth, cross-referenced:
1. `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sentinel_dispatches WHERE outcome = 'pending'` — DB count
2. Worktree existence check: for each pending dispatch, verify .worktrees/<agent_id> exists

If the DB says 'pending' but the worktree is gone, mark it 'orphaned' first (this is the collect phase doing cleanup).

### Enforcement point
In engine.rs, before dispatching each signal:
```rust
let in_flight = db.count_pending_dispatches()?;
if in_flight >= config.max_concurrent_agents {
    return Disposition::Defer { reason: "at capacity".into() };
}
```

Deferred signals are logged and retried on the next cycle. They are NOT recorded as dispatches — they stay eligible.

### Status integration
`crosslink sentinel status` should report:
- In-flight: N / max agents
- List each in-flight agent with: agent_id, signal_ref, started_at, elapsed time

### Edge case: capacity check race
Between the count query and the kickoff::run() call, another sentinel cycle (or manual kickoff) could spawn an agent. This is acceptable — the limit is a soft cap, not a hard semaphore. Overshooting by 1 occasionally is fine.

### Dependencies
- #651 (schema — count query)
- #654 (dispatch — enforcement point)
- #659 (daemon — status display)

ff3a9357-4fa0-4272-a49d-bc3c0105db03.author: dashboard-bootstrap
ff3a9357-4fa0-4272-a49d-bc3c0105db03.content: Implemented Option B (the author's recommendation) — self-healing invariant in configure_signing. New register_active_key_as_trusted() in sync/trust.rs reads the <key>.pub companion of whatever private key was just selected, checks allowed_signers via the new AllowedSigners::contains_key (dedupes by key body, ignoring trailing comments / different principals), appends an entry with principal '<agent_id>@crosslink' if absent, and commits unsigned (chicken-and-egg: the new key may not yet be visible in any earlier commit's allowed_signers view). Called after each of the three configure_signing branches (agent worktree, driver workspace, driver fallback) so the invariant 'the workspace's signer is always trusted' holds regardless of which path selected the key. Covers both the GH#585 fresh-bootstrap case AND the driver-key-rotation / re-init case the author flagged as Option B's advantage over Option A. 8 new tests: 5 for AllowedSigners::contains_key (exact match, trailing-comment-ignored, different body/type rejection, empty signers), 3 for configure_signing (driver-key registration with explicit user.signingkey override to avoid leaking the test host's real key, idempotency across 3 calls, graceful skip when .pub is missing). Full suite: 2850 passed (2842 baseline + 8 new). cargo fmt + clippy clean.

a1830ceb-941c-471f-886b-c38c84ea3b30.title: Dashboard InitBanner doesn't refresh on init success + track-all init fails on subset of repos

407b5c0f-acd5-416f-9658-164748f98b73.author: anon-ab6221f8
407b5c0f-acd5-416f-9658-164748f98b73.content: Shipped in 606c8e8a. Root cause: crosslink init --force short-circuits the 'Initializing database' step when .crosslink/.hub-cache/ already exists — exits 0 but leaves issues.db missing. Happened to ~half the track-all clones due to timing between the poll loop's ensure_hub_cache_worktree and the init subprocess. Fix: wipe_partial_crosslink_state now removes the hub-cache worktree properly (git worktree remove --force) AND nukes .crosslink/ entirely, so crosslink init sees a truly clean slate on retry. Hub-cache regenerates on the next poll tick via ensure_hub_cache_worktree. All 6 previously-broken repos retroactively initialised via POST /w/<slug>/init; all 15 tracked projects now write_capability=ready.

a2e2a376-dea7-4e68-a2c7-6f97aeaf2f9c.title: Phase 5.2: dashboard webhook alerting — outbound Slack/Discord/generic JSON on alert fire (GH #429)

9e25a164-7e9f-4bb1-8ce1-11e2ac9e17d6.author: anon-ab6221f8
9e25a164-7e9f-4bb1-8ce1-11e2ac9e17d6.content: Phase 5.2 shipped in 966f1002. Backend: webhook.rs (payload shapes for Slack/Discord/generic + reqwest dispatch w/ 5s timeout + URL masking + load/save_urls), webhook_api.rs (GET/PUT /api/v1/dashboard/webhooks). alerts_db::SyncStats gained opened_alerts: Vec<DerivedAlert> so poll.rs can iterate newly-fired alerts without a second DB query; poll_project spawns one fire-and-forget tokio task per (alert × URL). Frontend: /settings/webhooks page + useWebhooks/useSetWebhooks hooks + nav entry. 18 backend tests (13 webhook, 5 webhook_api) + 1 new alerts_db test + 7 vitest cases all passing; 56 frontend tests total, tsc + clippy clean. Deferred explicitly: email delivery (SMTP is too heavy — Slack/Discord/generic bridges cover it), per-URL test button, severity filters.

a8d1830f-a2e6-45d6-94c2-08b17eb17552.author: anon-ab6221f8
a8d1830f-a2e6-45d6-94c2-08b17eb17552.content:
Plan — Phase 5.2 scope and design:

Reuses sentinel/notify.rs's Slack Block Kit + generic JSON payload patterns, adapted for the dashboard's alert shape (kind/severity/subject_ref/detail/project_slug). Uses reqwest (already a dep) rather than shelling to curl.

Backend:
1. New crosslink/src/dashboard/webhook.rs module — WebhookPayload (Slack + generic variants), is_slack_url detection (hooks.slack.com), dispatch via reqwest, URL masking for logs.
2. Config persistence: webhook URL list JSON-encoded in dashboard.db 'config' table under key 'webhook.urls'.
3. alerts_db::sync_alerts_for_project return type extended to include the list of newly-opened DerivedAlert entries (not just counts) so poll.rs can dispatch.
4. Wire into poll.rs: after reconcile, if URLs configured, spawn task dispatching each new alert to each URL. Fire-and-forget with tracing::warn on failure.
5. REST endpoints /api/v1/dashboard/webhooks — GET (list) + PUT (replace full list).
6. Unit/axum-oneshot tests: payload shape, is_slack_url, dispatch_ok/err via mock server (tokio+hyper), round-trip GET/PUT, 400 without DB.

Frontend:
1. /settings/webhooks page — list stored URLs, add/remove, save.
2. API hooks (useWebhookConfig + useSetWebhookConfig).
3. vitest coverage.

Deferred: email (SMTP is too much state; Slack/Discord/generic bridges cover it), 'send test' button (nice-to-have, follow-up if this PR gets too big).

a47a4ad0-b961-425a-b73f-232af0d7f4b2.title: Alert lock actions: surface holder + disambiguate Release vs Steal

8787b691-de56-4b94-a048-699402d22b70.author: anon-ab6221f8
8787b691-de56-4b94-a048-699402d22b70.content:
Shipped in b9926fb8. Three clustered complaints:

1. Lock Release vs Steal confusion — Alert detail now shows 'held by <agent>' + 'claimed <timestamp>' from useProject(slug), plus a semantic hint: 'Use Release only if you're the holder. Use Steal to take over a stale lock held by another agent.' User won't click Release blind anymore.

2. Post-action invisibility — actions::run_cli now fast-forwards the hub-cache worktree (git reset --hard crosslink/hub) right after crosslink sync, so the frontend's mutation-triggered refetch sees the new state immediately instead of waiting for the next 5s poll tick. Applies to every write action (close/release/steal/comment/sign-backfill/etc). Skips reset if the hub-cache is dirty, same safety as poll::ensure_hub_cache_worktree.

3. signature_invalid now has an actionable button — new POST /integrity/sign-backfill endpoint runs 'crosslink integrity sign-backfill --confirm' via run_cli. Frontend useSignBackfill hook + 'Run sign-backfill' button on the alert card.

2 new vitest cases (lock-holder display, sign-backfill dispatch); full suite 103/103.

b957a8bc-0fd9-49c4-b804-8fc69195a6b0.author: anon-ab6221f8
b957a8bc-0fd9-49c4-b804-8fc69195a6b0.content:
## What

Implement `crosslink sentinel history` — shows past sentinel runs and their dispatch outcomes.

### Output format (table, default)
```
Run              Started              Signals  Dispatched  Collected  Skipped
sentinel-a1b2c3  2026-04-10 14:30:00  3        2           1          1
sentinel-d4e5f6  2026-04-10 14:20:00  1        0           0          1
```

With --json: array of run objects including nested dispatches array.

### Implementation
- Query sentinel_runs ORDER BY started_at DESC LIMIT <limit>
- For each run, optionally join sentinel_dispatches for detail view
- Format as table (default) or JSON (--json flag)
- Support --limit flag (default 10)

### Dependencies
- #651 (schema — sentinel_runs table and query methods)

af66aebd-bce5-4a3f-941a-0fd2911e6aa7.title: Fix sessions_new migration batch error on hub sync

12bf8baf-5710-466b-bde9-4c796d0b5f63.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
12bf8baf-5710-466b-bde9-4c796d0b5f63.content: Completed: PR #141 merged — sessions_new migration fix (wrong pragma column name)

226ebd18-548b-40ed-b465-d9867ef04695.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
226ebd18-548b-40ed-b465-d9867ef04695.content: Plan: (1) Fix the column name in the version query from 'version' to 'user_version'. (2) Make the v7 migration idempotent by dropping sessions_new if it exists before recreating it, and using explicit column names in the INSERT. (3) Clean up sessions_new in a new migration step.

28463f9c-b500-4aa5-86a8-56f1ce14af46.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
28463f9c-b500-4aa5-86a8-56f1ce14af46.content: Found root cause: db.rs:137 queries 'SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM pragma_user_version' but the column name in pragma_user_version is 'user_version', not 'version'. The query always fails with 'no such column', unwrap_or(0) returns 0, and the entire migration block runs on every Database::open(). This causes the v7 sessions_new migration to re-run every time, failing because sessions already has 7 columns (from v8/v9) but sessions_new only has 5.

6592b583-6e49-4999-9fe3-8fa47a1b1a60.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
6592b583-6e49-4999-9fe3-8fa47a1b1a60.content: Found root cause: db.rs:137 queries 'SELECT COALESCE(MAX(version), 0) FROM pragma_user_version' but the column name in pragma_user_version is 'user_version', not 'version'. The query always fails with 'no such column', unwrap_or(0) returns 0, and the entire migration block runs on every Database::open(). This causes the v7 sessions_new migration to re-run every time, failing because sessions already has 7 columns (from v8/v9) but sessions_new only has 5.

d25b4740-c25f-4515-8856-bfdb1d0d4715.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether
d25b4740-c25f-4515-8856-bfdb1d0d4715.content: Completed: PR #141 merged — sessions_new migration fix (wrong pragma column name)

dd736559-73da-4b59-b090-e1c0c42505f8.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--fix-sessions-migration
dd736559-73da-4b59-b090-e1c0c42505f8.content: Decision: Fix the column name (primary fix) AND make v7 migration robust (defensive fix). The column name fix prevents re-running migrations on every open. The v7 robustness fix ensures that even if the migration does re-run (e.g., from a DB at user_version < 7), it handles the case where sessions has extra columns. Also dropping leftover sessions_new table.

b1c329f6-be68-452a-8940-a2d10b05544a.title: Fix GH#584: worktree .git unresolvable in container + allowedTools blocks build tools

5bddf7ad-2aa1-4dda-9858-6bca7c83bdcf.author: dashboard-bootstrap
5bddf7ad-2aa1-4dda-9858-6bca7c83bdcf.content: Session auto-ended (stale after 1567 minutes). No handoff notes provided.

b26d1737-3976-4e7a-bb40-e02a2f456296.author: dashboard-bootstrap
b26d1737-3976-4e7a-bb40-e02a2f456296.content: Two-bug fix landed in one commit. Issue 1: launch_container takes a new host_repo_root parameter and adds a bind mount '-v <host_repo>/.git:<host_repo>/.git:rw' so the worktree's '.git' file (containing absolute 'gitdir: <host>/.git/worktrees/<branch>/' pointer) resolves inside the container. rw chosen because the per-worktree subdir under .git/worktrees/<branch>/ legitimately needs writes for commits; hook policy still blocks destructive ops. Issue 2 (chose Recommended): (a) detect_conventions in helpers.rs gains a has_manifest helper that scans the repo_root and one directory level deep for build manifests, skipping SKIP_SCAN_DIRS (node_modules, target, vendor, all hidden dirs, etc.) — catches the monorepo layout where Cargo.toml/pyproject.toml lives one level deep. Removed the special-cased crosslink/Cargo.toml check (the generic scan subsumes it). Added Bash(pytest *) to the Python auto-detect since GH#584 explicitly named it. (b) new read_kickoff_allowed_tools helper reads kickoff.allowed_tools array from hook-config.json, called from run.rs and appended to conventions.allowed_tools — purely additive override knob. New config key registered in config_registry.rs (Agents group, hot-swappable, StringArray). Documented in hook-config.qmd. Tests: 9 new (rust-one-level, rust-two-levels-rejected, python-subdir-with-pytest, node_modules skip, hidden-dir skip, plus 4 for read_kickoff_allowed_tools covering missing/present/absent-key/malformed). Full suite: 2817 passed (was 2808 + 9 new). cargo fmt + clippy clean.

b42e49af-f559-4de5-8fba-64a884bb1efc.title: Fix piped shell commands in skill templates that fail permission checks

604e4e28-891b-4658-a65a-c02cbd152398.author: maxine--basel
604e4e28-891b-4658-a65a-c02cbd152398.content: Identified all problematic patterns across 7 skill files. The issue is pipes (|) and OR-fallbacks (||) in dynamic context expansions. Strategy: replace pipes with single commands, and replace || fallbacks with 2>/dev/null alone (empty output is fine, better than a permission error).

e4ddec60-a11d-4a00-a542-7af140d811ad.author: maxine--basel
e4ddec60-a11d-4a00-a542-7af140d811ad.content: Plan: Replace all piped/multi-operation shell commands in skill template context blocks with simple single commands. The Bash permission checker rejects multi-part commands (pipes, ||, &&) in dynamic context expansion. Will rewrite each to use a single command or remove the dynamic expansion where a static fallback suffices.

b60908ce-3b37-4a6e-851f-b0b626471ab1.title: Fix dashboard auth: wire API client to bearer token (GH#556)

49f877d7-bca9-4f92-b059-151becd41826.author: anon-ab6221f8
49f877d7-bca9-4f92-b059-151becd41826.content: Root cause: server's auth_middleware (src/server/mod.rs:30-54) requires Authorization: Bearer <token> on every /api/* route except /api/v1/health and /ws. Dashboard client (dashboard/src/api/client.ts) exports configureApiClient but nothing ever calls it, so requests go out without the header and every call 401s. Fix: new module dashboard/src/auth/bootstrap.ts runs before React mounts, reads ?token= from URL, persists to sessionStorage, scrubs URL via history.replaceState, and wires configureApiClient with a fetch wrapper that injects the header. Also: serve banner now prints a clickable Dashboard: http://addr/?token=<token> URL when --dashboard-dir is set. Tests: 7 new vitest cases.

b93375ab-94ea-4138-9e95-2aca9b413a32.title: Restore agent/human-driver hook separation broken by auto-agent-init in crosslink init (see #566)

93e45d0b-1800-4c92-988b-af1141d927a0.author: anon-ab6221f8
93e45d0b-1800-4c92-988b-af1141d927a0.content: Root cause: commit be839515 (2026-03-30, 'fix: consistent signing bypass for all hub-cache commits', closes #529) made crosslink init auto-write .crosslink/agent.json for hub-cache signing. is_agent_context() (introduced 2026-03-06 in 2b221468) treats agent.json presence as proof the session is a kickoff agent — an invariant that held until be839515 repurposed agent.json as a signing identity file written to main repos too. Plan: split identity from session-role via a role field on AgentConfig. crosslink init writes role=driver (signing only, not an agent). crosslink agent init (kickoff) writes role=agent. is_agent_context parses agent.json and only returns True on role=agent; cwd-in-.claude/worktrees/ kept as secondary signal for Claude-Code sub-agents. Backward-compat: missing role deserializes to None → treated as driver, which is correct for all existing main-repo agent.json files auto-created by crosslink init.

caf53613-a38b-4a57-900e-2d68348c1a0b.author: anon-ab6221f8
caf53613-a38b-4a57-900e-2d68348c1a0b.content: Implementation done. Split AgentConfig identity from session-role: added AgentRole enum (Driver default, Agent). AgentConfig::init keeps driver semantics; new init_with_role(Agent) used by agent init + bootstrap. Python is_agent_context reads role and only returns True on role=agent (plus .claude/worktrees/ cwd signal preserved). Legacy/malformed agent.json defaults to driver (safe). Verified end-to-end against work-check.py: no-agent-json + role=driver + legacy-no-role + malformed all block git push (exit 2), role=agent allows (exit 0). 1697 lib tests pass including 5 new role tests. Drive-by: fixed flaky identity proptest that could generate Windows-reserved names validate() rejects.

b94eac94-8bc1-4921-aec2-e1aed598fce0.title: Clarify that agents/containers are optional for solo use
b94eac94-8bc1-4921-aec2-e1aed598fce0.description: New users are immediately confused by agent identity, SSH keys, containers, and locks — features designed for multi-agent coordination. Daniel Lowd asked 'Do I have agents? Do I need agents? What is the SSH key for? Should I be putting things in Docker containers?' For single-user workflows these are all optional but nothing in the UX signals that. Need to either: (a) defer agent init until multi-agent features are actually needed, (b) clearly label agent/container features as 'multi-agent only' in help text, or (c) add a simpler init path that skips agent identity. Ref: bsky thread lowd.cc/3mh4vf3fias2x

b9a29156-1892-4615-9344-8877cebe9dfc.title: Fix TOCTOU race in compaction lock file retry path

b4bf9b94-70cc-4a44-a621-d853900b3953.author: anon-ab6221f8
b4bf9b94-70cc-4a44-a621-d853900b3953.content: Simplify the lock acquisition: first try_create with create_new(true) is already atomic. If it fails with AlreadyExists, go to stale-lock check. If it fails for any other reason, propagate the error. Remove the double-create pattern.

f3d2ccbb-bbd9-4d2d-b1a5-5e0856164401.author: anon-ab6221f8
f3d2ccbb-bbd9-4d2d-b1a5-5e0856164401.content: Replaced double-create pattern with single create + error classification. Transient filesystem errors (permissions, disk full) now propagate instead of falling through to stale-lock logic. AlreadyExists (file exists) correctly falls through to staleness check. Eliminates TOCTOU window between exists check and retry. All 1665 tests pass.

f020afd1-383b-4ce0-adbf-f7443649d471.author: anon-ab6221f8
f020afd1-383b-4ce0-adbf-f7443649d471.content:
## What

Create the  module directory structure and wire up the CLI subcommands. This is the skeleton that every other issue builds on.

### Files to create
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/mod.rs` — `SentinelCommands` enum (Run, Watch, Status, History, Stop) + `dispatch()` function
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/engine.rs` — empty `run_oneshot()` and `watch()` stubs that return Ok
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/config.rs` — `SentinelConfig` struct + `load()` from hook-config.json
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/sources/mod.rs` — `Source` trait, `SourceKind` enum, `SignalKind` enum, `Signal` struct
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/sources/github.rs` — empty `GitHubLabelSource` stub
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/dispatch.rs` — `Disposition` enum, `AgentScope` struct, empty `triage()`
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/collect.rs` — empty `collect_completed()` stub
- `crosslink/src/commands/sentinel/history.rs` — empty `show_history()` stub

### Integration points
- Add `pub mod sentinel;` to `commands/mod.rs`
- Add `Sentinel { action: SentinelCommands }` variant to the `Commands` enum in `main.rs` (~line 105)
- Add match arm in the main dispatch (~line 2678): call `commands::sentinel::dispatch()`
- `sentinel run --dry-run` should print 'sentinel not yet implemented' and exit 0 (placeholder)

### Why this is first
Every other sentinel issue imports from these modules. Getting the skeleton wired up and compiling means all subsequent work can be done in parallel without merge conflicts on module declarations.

### Acceptance criteria
- `crosslink sentinel run` compiles and runs (prints placeholder)
- `crosslink sentinel run --dry-run` works
- `crosslink sentinel watch`, `status`, `history`, `stop` all compile
- `cargo test` passes (no regressions)
- Module structure matches the design doc layout

be1de53c-1e60-44be-bc9d-6727e5fce489.title: Support rules.local directory for gitignored local rule overrides

5b7c5705-0eae-4f3e-a5fe-c1bb52bf756e.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--rules-local
5b7c5705-0eae-4f3e-a5fe-c1bb52bf756e.content:
Plan: 6 areas to modify:
1. init.rs GITIGNORE_MANAGED_SECTION — add .crosslink/rules.local/ to gitignore
2. init.rs .crosslink/.gitignore — add rules.local/ entry
3. init.rs init logic — create rules.local/ directory during init
4. prompt-guard.py load_all_rules() — merge rules.local/ over rules/ (same-name override, additive for new files)
5. prompt-guard.py load_tracking_rules() — check rules.local/ first
6. pre-web-check.py load_web_rules() — check rules.local/ first
7. context.rs — show rules.local/ files in context measure
8. workflow.rs — skip drift detection for rules.local/ (they're intentional overrides)

Key design decision: rules.local/ files override same-named rules/ files entirely (no merging within a file). New files in rules.local/ are additive.

c156deb9-0f3a-42e6-b52a-da3e95109c5f.title: hub-v3 PR2: plumbing write path to per-agent refs behind hub_v3 flag
c156deb9-0f3a-42e6-b52a-da3e95109c5f.description: Second PR of the hub v3 migration (.design/hub-v3-per-agent-refs.md, REQ-1, REQ-2, REQ-8). Implement event writes via git plumbing (hash-object/mktree/commit-tree/update-ref) to refs/crosslink/agents/<agent-id> with fast-forward-only push semantics, gated behind a hub_v3 config flag. Single unified local lock per REQ-8. Dual-write soak in the crosslink repo itself. Acceptance: AC-1 (100-iteration two-agent concurrency, zero conflicts), AC-2 (crash-injection harness over the plumbing write path).

0736c9d0-2d97-40c2-a017-775f687f56b6.author: dashboard-bootstrap
0736c9d0-2d97-40c2-a017-775f687f56b6.content: Plan (dual-write shape, agreed with user): (1) New module src/hub_v3.rs: RefWriter using git plumbing only -- agent_ref_name() = refs/crosslink/agents/<id>; append_event_to_ref(): read tip via rev-parse (absent = genesis), read events.log blob from tip, append NDJSON line, hash-object -w, mktree, commit-tree -p tip, update-ref WITH old-value CAS (local compare-and-swap); push_agent_ref(): plain non-force refspec push = remote CAS. cfg(test) fault-injection point for crash harness. (2) Config key hub_v3.dual_write (default false) in config_registry following existing key read patterns. (3) Integration at the SINGLE production append site, shared_writer/core.rs emit_compact_push: after append_event, if flag on, mirror envelope to own agent ref + push best-effort -- shadow failures tracing::warn and never fail the user operation. (4) crosslink integrity hubv3: parity check, shadow-ref event stream must be a suffix-subset of the v2 log matched by (agent_id, agent_seq) with byte-identical envelopes; PASS/FAIL verdict for soak monitoring. State-level parity deferred to PR3 when genesis migration seeds full history. (5) Tests: RefWriter unit (append/genesis/CAS-conflict), crash-injection abort-at-each-step (ref is old-or-new, next write succeeds), two-agent same-repo concurrency (zero conflicts), dual-write smoke via SharedWriter, parity-detects-tampering. REQ-8 single-lock unification deferred to PR4 (shadow writes run under the existing hub write lock already held in emit_compact_push). Soak: flag off by default; user opts in locally post-merge; smoke test exercises parity in CI via cargo test.

253bd064-f9ed-4ee8-a5c6-193cbb2e1d63.author: dashboard-bootstrap
253bd064-f9ed-4ee8-a5c6-193cbb2e1d63.content: Committed ea272b13 on feature/752-hub-v3-write-path. Both implementation agents completed; director review found and fixed two defects in the parity check before commit: (1) false-FAIL after crosslink prune -- shadow events absent from v2 are now tolerated iff covered by the checkpoint watermark, with a regression test covering both the covered-PASS and uncovered-FAIL directions; (2) process::exit(1) buried in check_hubv3_parity made the FAIL path untestable and the agent's tamper test was vacuous (asserted bytes differ without calling the check) -- replaced with bail!/Err per integrity command conventions and rewrote the test to assert the check itself errors. Gates: fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 1935 lib + 2924 bin tests passing (+20 total). Soak: crosslink config set hub_v3.dual_write true locally post-merge, monitor via crosslink integrity hubv3 and .crosslink/hub-v3-shadow-stats.json. Awaiting push.

58076e3f-8d0a-4720-bb26-988065a9f716.author: dashboard-bootstrap
58076e3f-8d0a-4720-bb26-988065a9f716.content: PASS 2 complete. Mode seam: HubMode{V2,V3} (hub_v3.rs ~620), HubMode::resolve(cache_dir) called once in SyncManager::new (sync/core.rs); SharedWriter reads via sync.hub_mode(). V3 prepare skips counter claims: claim_display_id/claim_milestone_id early-return (0, Counters::default()) when is_v3 (no counters.json read); normalize_events_for_v3 strips display_id->None on IssueCreated/CommentAdded/TimeEntryAdded/MilestoneCreated (shared_writer/core.rs). write_commit_push + emit_compact_push branch to commit_v3 (append_event_to_ref own ref -> push_agent_ref FF -> fetch+adopt others lock-free -> reduce/cache state -> write+push checkpoint, NO prune). read_max_event_seq mode-aware: V3 reads max agent_seq from own ref via hub_v3::read_max_event_seq_from_ref. lock_check v3: read_locks_auto->read_locks_v3 reads LOCAL checkpoint ref state.json directly (no full reduce); staleness window = last local compaction (== v2 read_locks_auto reads last-compact materialized files). Fetch adoption: other agents' refs adopted unconditionally to remote tip (writer-authoritative, FF or post-prune non-FF); own ref never moved from remote; checkpoint adopted when remote watermark>=local; fetch refreshes local checkpoint but does NOT prune (prune confined to compact cmd which force-syncs own ref after prune). hydrate_from_state(state,&db) reuses db.insert_hydrated_* helpers, single txn, FK-off, empty-state data-loss guard, #443 SQLite-only preservation. Heartbeats/requests/acks routed to hub_v3 primitives when V3. Entry points mode-routed: sync_cmd, daemon tick, compact cmd. Idempotency guard added to compact_v3 (skip checkpoint commit when state.json byte-identical) to keep migrate re-run a no-op. Direct-reader sites for PASS 3 (unchanged, routed via read_locks_auto/read_heartbeats_auto which are now mode-aware so they work, but these read state directly): server/watcher.rs:114,164; dashboard/reader.rs:151-164,309,355,366 (reads issue files/locks.json/heartbeats DIRECTLY - NOT via read_*_auto, needs v3 rework); tui/agents_tab.rs:177,182,867,876; tui/config_tab.rs:1125,1131; server/handlers/agents.rs:201,205,260,266,386,440; server/handlers/sync.rs:44; commands/next.rs:32; commands/agent.rs:748; commands/locks_cmd.rs list/check; integrity hubv3 unchanged. Tests: lib 1996(+3 hydrate_from_state), bin 3001(+11 v3 e2e), cli 194. Gates: fmt clean, both clippy clean.

e4930d44-0bf7-49eb-aed9-5f5f21367793.author: dashboard-bootstrap
e4930d44-0bf7-49eb-aed9-5f5f21367793.content: PASS 2 plan. Add HubMode{V2,V3} in hub_v3.rs, resolve once via detect_hub_version(cache_dir) on SyncManager construction (new field hub_mode). SharedWriter reads sync.hub_mode(). Seams: (1) write_commit_push/emit_compact_push branch to commit_v3 when V3 — take WriteSet.events only, normalize display_id->None on IssueCreated/CommentAdded/TimeEntryAdded/MilestoneCreated, envelope via append_envelopes-equivalent but seq from own-ref log, append_event_to_ref + push_agent_ref (FF, push failure benign LocalOnly), compact_v3 + hydrate_from_state, return reduction-assigned id from state.display_id_map. claim_display_id/claim_milestone_id mode-aware: V3 returns (0, default) with NO counter read. read_max_event_seq mode-aware: V3 reads max agent_seq from own ref events.log via git cat-file. (2) hydrate_from_state(state,&db) in hydration.rs reusing db.insert_hydrated_* helpers, single txn, #443 preserve + empty-state guard. (3) fetch V3: fetch checkpoint(+force) + agents/* into crosslink-remote/*, adopt other-agent refs to remote tip (writer-authoritative), own ref never moved, checkpoint adopt by watermark, compact_v3 + hydrate_from_state. (4) lock_check V3: read LOCAL checkpoint ref state.json directly (no full reduce) -> state.locks (cheap, documented staleness = last local compact). (5) heartbeats/requests route to hub_v3 primitives when V3. (6) entry points sync/daemon/compact mode-route. Dual_write IGNORED in V3.

c85b4143-be7c-45ad-a7e1-f6faf1c1dc75.title: Fix CI concurrency conflict between push and PR triggers on release branches

0bbc1630-92fc-4523-b3d8-2602212b2d20.author: maxine--basel
0bbc1630-92fc-4523-b3d8-2602212b2d20.content: Push to release/* and PR to develop both trigger ci.yml. Concurrency group resolves to same key (CI-release/v0.5.0) for both events via github.head_ref || github.ref_name. cancel-in-progress: true cancels the first run, leaving canceled checks that block merge. Fix: remove release/** from push triggers since PR trigger already covers it.

cd21c374-bdd9-4c8e-b3e1-d59bfedc6d4b.title: Harden hub sync: unique atomic_write temps + fsync, remove live-holder lock force-steal, unify worktree locking

6de56294-b0f6-4244-b9c5-6a977f9cda72.author: dashboard-bootstrap
6de56294-b0f6-4244-b9c5-6a977f9cda72.content: Design doc for hub v3 written to .design/hub-v3-per-agent-refs.md: per-agent refs (refs/crosslink/agents/<id>, always-fast-forward pushes), pure event sourcing (counters.json and locks.json eliminated), plumbing-based writes (no shared worktree), checkpoint as force-with-lease cache ref, single unified local lock, 4-PR migration sequence with full-state-diff gate. Lock stampede semantics preserved via first-claim-wins + claim-confirm; enforcement model documented as hook-gate + signed attribution + merge gate.

dd1dd3e0-061b-4166-a6d6-c359c9225a8c.author: dashboard-bootstrap
dd1dd3e0-061b-4166-a6d6-c359c9225a8c.content: Committed 8abe7c01 on fix/hub-sync-hardening: all four option-A fixes implemented and verified (cargo fmt clean, clippy --all-targets -D warnings clean, 1906 lib tests passing including 2 new regression tests for concurrent atomic_write and live-holder lock timeout). rewrite_as_offline caller trace confirmed sequential (not nested) lock acquisition, no restructuring needed. Design doc .design/hub-v3-per-agent-refs.md included in same commit. Awaiting push by user, then PR to develop.

e0919892-0f4f-41de-b85b-3e50ab4f3557.author: dashboard-bootstrap
e0919892-0f4f-41de-b85b-3e50ab4f3557.content: Plan: (1) utils::atomic_write — unique temp names via tempfile::NamedTempFile (already a dependency) + fsync file before rename + fsync parent dir on Unix; (2) sync/cache.rs acquire_hub_lock — stop force-removing the lock of a LIVE holder after 30s timeout, bail with actionable error instead (keep dead-PID stale removal); (3) unify locking so compaction's worktree materialization cannot race write_commit_push — compaction must hold the hub write lock, with care for the emit_compact_push path that already holds it (avoid self-deadlock); (4) shared_writer/mutations.rs rewrite_as_offline — replace std::fs::write with atomic_write (same bug class as GH#604). Plus .design/hub-v3-per-agent-refs.md design doc for the event-sourcing/per-agent-refs migration discussed in session. Findings sourced from hub corruption audit: non-unique .basename.tmp collision (utils.rs:131), live-holder force-steal (cache.rs:88-96), disjoint compaction.lock vs .hub-write-lock domains.

cd68bf43-c841-4ec1-ae8f-37632d68765f.title: Fix test_configure_signing_with_key regression from role-aware signing

8a59e288-16d0-4650-a8b9-4f320eecc7be.author: dashboard-bootstrap
8a59e288-16d0-4650-a8b9-4f320eecc7be.content: Root cause: role-aware configure_signing went driver→disable when user.signingkey wasn't set in main repo. Test_configure_signing_with_key asserts gpg.format=ssh with only agent.json keys. Fix: after driver_signing_key() miss, fall back to agent.ssh_key_path if present. Semantics still role-aware — driver key wins when configured — but operators without user.signingkey don't get unsigned hub commits.

Shipped in 839e1546. Two fixes:

1. run_init_and_agent_in — with_context now includes resolved binary path + workspace path on spawn failure, and non-zero-exit paths surface trimmed stderr AND stdout (so users can distinguish 'binary missing' vs 'workspace gone' vs 'subprocess printed its own error').

d39b301a-60f6-46c0-8b16-bf3809304fb6.title: Fix: crosslink init bootstrap leaves no pending key (trust enforcement stuck)

84882ac3-0dac-4873-b49e-177734f52ee3.author: dashboard-bootstrap
84882ac3-0dac-4873-b49e-177734f52ee3.content:
Fix on branch fix/738-bootstrap-auto-complete-on-register (off develop). Changes:
1. sync/trust.rs: register_active_key_as_trusted now returns Result<bool> and, when it adds a new entry while bootstrap.status=='pending', calls complete_bootstrap() and stages meta/bootstrap.json into the same unsigned commit.
2. sync/bootstrap.rs: added 'trust: register signing key' to BOOTSTRAP_MESSAGE_PREFIXES so the unsigned self-trust commit is correctly filtered by signing audit.
3. commands/locks_cmd.rs sync_cmd: snapshot bootstrap state before/after configure_signing and print 'Bootstrap complete — signing enforcement is now active.' (parity with trust approve).
4. sync/tests.rs: two new unit tests prove (a) first configure_signing call flips bootstrap pending->complete and writes completed_at, (b) subsequent calls leave completed_at unchanged.
Verification: cargo build clean; cargo fmt clean; cargo clippy clean; 145 sync:: tests pass.

8fc6be33-3c5a-4bed-ae08-d90b64adeda6.author: dashboard-bootstrap
8fc6be33-3c5a-4bed-ae08-d90b64adeda6.content: Root cause: PR #597 (GH#585) added register_active_key_as_trusted() in crosslink/src/sync/trust.rs:610, which runs every configure_signing() call and silently auto-adds the workspace signing key into allowed_signers as <agent_id>@crosslink. Because trust pending classifies a key as 'approved' if its principal is in allowed_signers, every published key shows as approved — yet complete_bootstrap() is only called from crosslink trust approve (commands/trust.rs:99). Result: meta/bootstrap.json stays 'pending' forever; with signing_enforcement='enforced' every sync bails with instructions that are impossible to follow (trust pending shows nothing, no agent-id to approve). Secondary: the new commit message 'trust: register signing key for ...' is NOT in BOOTSTRAP_MESSAGE_PREFIXES (sync/bootstrap.rs:29-34), so post-fix it would fail the enforcement audit.

d579d744-14f0-48cf-9335-0fc8983bb221.title: Phase 5.3: dashboard polish — theme toggle + audible alerts + preferences page (GH #429)

a2a0d9b3-3af4-4ee1-b6f9-b1643eedd172.author: anon-ab6221f8
a2a0d9b3-3af4-4ee1-b6f9-b1643eedd172.content:
Plan — Phase 5.3 polish scope:

Three features, unified on a new /settings/preferences page:

1. Theme toggle — light/dark/system (follow media query). Palette lives in index.css; light mode added as a class override. Persisted to localStorage via a tiny module-level store with a subscribe() hook. Also exposed as a one-click toggle button in the top nav.

2. Audible alerts — plays a short WebAudio tone when a dashboard_alerts_changed WS event arrives with opened > 0. Severity-filtered (critical always, warning optional, info never by default). No external MP3 — oscillator-synthesized tones to keep the bundle small and avoid asset-pipeline fuss. Toggle + severity filter exposed on the preferences page; disabled by default (opt-in).

3. Preferences page — the unifying UI. Theme radio + audible toggle + severity checkboxes. No 'custom dashboards' tile-drag thing (that's design-doc-vague and a rabbit hole; scoped out explicitly).

Mechanics:
- lib/preferences.ts: typed localStorage-backed store + React subscription hook (useSyncExternalStore). Single source of truth.
- lib/theme.ts: applies theme class to documentElement on change + media-query listener for system mode.
- lib/alertSound.ts: WebAudio oscillator player + subscribe-to-WS helper.
- api/ws.ts: add a module-level event bus for alert events so non-queryClient consumers can react.
- pages/SettingsPreferences.tsx: the page.
- App.tsx: mount a ThemeBootstrap + AlertSound bridge, add nav + route.

Tests:
- preferences store roundtrip (vitest)
- useTheme applies the right class and listens to system changes
- alertSound plays on WS event that matches severity filter; stays silent otherwise
- SettingsPreferences renders all controls, toggles persist
- No backend changes this phase — pure frontend polish.

Deferred: custom dashboard tile layouts (too vague; would want a separate design pass), per-project mute, notification-snooze windows.

f5beb1bb-14db-4f89-9fa9-dd211d9a1cd5.author: anon-ab6221f8
f5beb1bb-14db-4f89-9fa9-dd211d9a1cd5.content:
Phase 5.3 shipped. Three frontend polish features landed in one commit:

• Theme: dark (default) + light palette via .theme-light override in index.css, lib/theme.ts resolves 'system' against prefers-color-scheme with live OS-flip tracking, one-click ☀/☾ toggle in the top nav.

• Audible alerts: WebAudio-synthesised tones (severity-specific: A4/D5/G5) via lib/alertSound.ts — no MP3 assets, zero bundle growth. Plays via api/ws.ts's new subscribeAlertOpens bus on dashboard_alerts_changed WS fires where opened>0 and severity matches the user's filter.

• Preferences page: /settings/preferences with theme radio + audible toggle + severity checkboxes. lib/preferences.ts stores settings in localStorage with useSyncExternalStore-backed React hook, canonical-order dedupe on save, sanitization + tolerance for corrupted JSON on load.

Explicit scope cuts: 'custom dashboards' (too vague for a single-PR implementation — needs its own design pass), per-project mute, notification snooze windows.

Tests: 25 new vitest cases across 4 files (preferences store, theme observer incl. media-listener lifecycle, alertSound bridge, SettingsPreferences UI). Full suite: 81/81 pass, tsc clean. No backend changes this phase.

d684b80e-23b2-4abd-b550-2a213d1c7096.title: Fix GH#586: init should write tracker_remote + classify push errors better

f9257341-9545-46dd-8727-7599d2b96349.author: dashboard-bootstrap
f9257341-9545-46dd-8727-7599d2b96349.content: All three fixes landed in one commit per scope choice. (1) hook-config.json embedded template gains tracker_remote: 'origin' so the value is visible on disk; init.rs's new populate_tracker_remote() detects git remotes via 'git remote' and upgrades from 'origin' to a non-origin remote when the template default doesn't match reality. Decision matrix preserves byte-equality with the embedded template in the matches-reality case (so workflow diff --check keeps passing). Manual non-default values are short-circuited at the top so init --force never clobbers them. (2) Structured PushFailure classifier in sync/mod.rs with Auth → RemoteMisconfigured → NonFastForward → Offline → Other ordering (Auth checked first because failed auth often emits 'Could not read from remote' too — Offline would have miscategorized). All three push call sites (sync/cache.rs, sync/heartbeats.rs, shared_writer/core.rs) now share the classifier. (3) Per user choice 'warn for offline, error for misconfigured': tracing::warn! for Offline preserves existing UX; tracing::error! for RemoteMisconfigured/AuthFailed/Other with PushFailure::user_message() rendering actionable guidance ('crosslink config set tracker_remote ...', 'crosslink sync', 'check SSH key'). 22 new tests (11 classifier covering all 5 buckets + ordering edge case, 11 init helper covering detect_git_remotes + populate_tracker_remote decision matrix including byte-equality preservation). Full suite: 2842 passed (2820 baseline + 22 new). cargo fmt + clippy clean.

db141eaf-3152-437a-9f89-24e7eb97ab3f.title: V0: dispatch engine — triage + kickoff integration

95bede1e-8460-49a3-903f-aaf29f3af3fb.author: anon-ab6221f8
95bede1e-8460-49a3-903f-aaf29f3af3fb.content:
## What

Implement the dispatch engine — the core of sentinel. Takes signals from sources, runs them through triage rules, and spawns kickoff agents.

### Triage Engine
`fn triage(signal: &Signal, decision: SignalDecision, config: &SentinelConfig) -> Disposition`

V0 has one hardcoded rule:
- `agent-todo: replicate` -> Disposition::Dispatch with:
  - allowed_paths: ["tests/"]
  - verify: VerifyLevel::Local
  - timeout: 30 minutes (Sonnet) or 45 minutes (Opus escalation, 1.5x multiplier)
  - model: Sonnet for attempt 1, Opus for attempt 2

Unrecognized labels -> Disposition::Skip.

### Dispatch via kickoff::run::run()

The integration point is building a KickoffOpts struct and calling `kickoff::run::run(crosslink_dir, db, writer, &opts)`. Key fields:
- `description`: The scoped reproduction prompt (see design doc, Agent Prompt section)
- `issue`: The crosslink issue ID (created during dispatch)
- `verify`: VerifyLevel::Local
- `model`: from triage decision
- `timeout`: from triage decision
- `dry_run`: false (real dispatch)
- `container`: ContainerMode::None (local tmux)
- `skip_permissions`: false

### Reproduction prompt
The prompt must be tightly scoped:
1. Read the GH issue
2. Write a failing test
3. Run the test suite
4. Record findings via crosslink comments
5. May ONLY modify tests/ directories
6. May NOT fix the bug or push code

The prompt is built as a string and passed as `opts.description`.

### Capacity check
Before dispatching, count in-flight agents:
`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM sentinel_dispatches WHERE outcome = 'pending'`
If >= max_concurrent_agents, return Disposition::Defer.

### Transaction flow
1. Create crosslink issue for the signal
2. Label it 'sentinel' + 'bug'
3. Check capacity
4. Build KickoffOpts
5. Call kickoff::run::run() -> get compact_name
6. Insert sentinel_dispatches record with agent_id = compact_name, outcome = 'pending'

If step 5 fails, record dispatch with outcome = 'failure' and continue to next signal.

### engine.rs: run_oneshot()
Orchestrates a single sentinel cycle:
1. Load config
2. Check enabled
3. Initialize sources
4. Load SeenSet from DB
5. Poll all sources
6. Triage each signal
7. Dispatch eligible signals
8. Collect results from previously completed agents (#655)
9. Record run stats in sentinel_runs
10. Print summary

### Dependencies
- #650 (module skeleton)
- #651 (schema — insert/query methods)
- #652 (source — signals to triage)
- #653 (dedup — SeenSet for triage decisions)
- kickoff::run::run() (existing, no changes needed)
- kickoff::launch::init_worktree_agent() (existing, no changes needed)

dd1202c5-651d-4596-b25d-65378d4e1812.title: init walkthrough writes '(text)' placeholder for all ConfigType::String keys (tracker_remote, sentinel models)
dd1202c5-651d-4596-b25d-65378d4e1812.description:
## Symptom

Every repo initialized after the init walkthrough TUI landed (commit unknown — predates #736) gets a corrupt hook-config.json with the literal string \"(text)\" as the value for every ConfigType::String key:
- tracker_remote: \"(text)\"
- sentinel.default_agent.model: \"(text)\"
- sentinel.escalation.model: \"(text)\"

In repos with a remote, this manifests as:
- crosslink sync prints \"Warning: push failed (offline)\" or \"error: push failed: remote '(text)' is not a valid git endpoint\"
- crosslink locks list shows stale L1 referencing unpromoted offline issue with id -1
- Local issues created before first sync stay at sentinel id -1 even after manual git push of crosslink/hub and crosslink/knowledge to origin
- crosslink init --force does NOT recover, because populate_tracker_remote() in init/mod.rs:347 treats anything ≠ \"origin\" as a manual edit

## Root cause

\`crosslink/src/commands/config_registry.rs:538-559\` build_config() materializes TUI selections into config values. For ConfigType::String, options_for_key() (line 445) returns vec![\"(text)\"] as a UI placeholder meaning \"this is a free-text field, not a cycle option.\" build_config()'s default match arm at line 552 then writes that placeholder verbatim into the config:

\`\`\`rust
let val = match entry.config_type {
    ConfigType::Bool => match val_str { ... },
    _ => serde_json::Value::String(val_str.to_string()),  // ← bug: writes \"(text)\" for String keys
};
\`\`\`

apply_tui_choices() in init/walkthrough.rs:643-645 then overwrites the template defaults (which were correct, e.g. \"origin\") with the corrupt values.

## Reproduction

1. crosslink init in any new repo with the walkthrough TUI
2. cat .crosslink/hook-config.json | grep tracker_remote → shows \"(text)\"

## Fix

In config_registry.rs build_config() at line ~547, either:
- Skip ConfigType::String entries (preserve template default)
- Or only insert when the user actually entered a value (the TUI has no text-input handler today, so the answer is \"never insert\")

Audit: also confirm none of the other corrupted String keys cause silent breakage. sentinel models with \"(text)\" will at minimum fail any later sentinel dispatch.

## Recovery for existing affected repos

\`\`\`
crosslink config set tracker_remote origin
crosslink config set sentinel.default_agent.model <model>
crosslink config set sentinel.escalation.model <model>
\`\`\`

Cannot be auto-fixed by init --force because populate_tracker_remote() refuses to touch non-\"origin\" values.

## Related

- #736 (GH#586): fixed the classifier and tracker_remote write-on-init logic, but did not touch build_config()
- #738 (GH#583): bootstrap auto-complete — orthogonal

343e32dc-dee1-42bb-a17a-8e85063d8f53.author: dashboard-bootstrap
343e32dc-dee1-42bb-a17a-8e85063d8f53.content:
Phase 2 of #604 event-sourcing migration. Builds on 8dffd394.

What landed:

12 more mutations now event-sourced (in addition to the 6 in phase 1):
  close_issue, reopen_issue, delete_issue, update_issue,
  add_comment, add_intervention_comment,
  create_milestone, close_milestone, delete_milestone,
  set_milestone_on_issues, clear_milestone_on_issue,
  (plus the 6 from phase 1: add/remove blocker/relation/label)

New event variants: IssueDeleted, CommentAdded, MilestoneCreated, MilestoneClosed, MilestoneDeleted, OfflinePromoted. IssueUpdated extended with clear_description, scheduled_at, due_at.

Checkpoint state extended: CompactMilestone, CompactComment, state.milestones, state.next_milestone_id.

Compaction extended: apply arms for new events, materialize for milestones and per-comment files (V2 layout), atomic_write everywhere, issue/milestone deletions, counters.json regeneration.

write_commit_push hardened: failure mode #1 closed — reset working tree on ANY commit failure (was only for use_git_rm). apply_write_set switched to atomic_write so failure mode #4 (truncated write) is closed too.

New: crosslink integrity hydration --replay-from-genesis (wipes checkpoint, recomputes state from every event in every agent log, rebuilds JSON view, hydrates SQLite).

New: crosslink integrity backfill-events (synthesizes events from legacy JSON-only workspaces so they can be migrated forward).

Still on write_commit_push: create_issue, create_subissue, promote_offline_issues. These have offline-mode complexity (negative display_id convention) that needs design separately from the transactionality fix. ac0d4bab-7159-420d-bf66-d1df48bd48f2.author: dashboard-bootstrap
ac0d4bab-7159-420d-bf66-d1df48bd48f2.content:
Phase 1 of #604: migrate the six relationship mutations (add/remove × {blocker, relation, label}) from the JSON-first write_commit_push path to the event-first emit_compact_push path.

The event-sourcing substrate already existed in crosslink — events.rs, signing, compaction, materialize — but ONLY the lock claim/release path used it. All issue mutations bypassed it, writing JSON files directly. That's the structural gap #604 calls out.

Changes:
1. Fixed latent compaction bug: materialize() was using From<&CompactIssue> for IssueFile which sets comments=[] and time_entries=[]. With locks-only events that never fired, but any non-lock event-driven materialize would silently wipe those fields. Now materialize merges with the existing JSON file on disk to preserve fields CompactIssue doesn't track.

2. Added From<&IssueFile> for CompactIssue + seed_issues_from_disk() pre-pass in compact() to bootstrap state.issues for UUIDs referenced by events but not yet in state — bridges the migration window from JSON-canonical to event-canonical.

3. Rewired 6 SharedWriter mutations: add_blocker, remove_blocker, add_relation, remove_relation, add_label, remove_label. Each is now ~10 lines instead of ~30: build event, emit_compact_push, hydrate. Compaction materializes the JSON view; event log is canonical.

Failure-mode improvements vs #604's table:
- Mode #1 (commit fails after JSON write): event-append is the atomic boundary now. JSON is written by compaction AFTER event lands. No JSON-only drift.
- Mode #4 (truncated JSON write): materialize already uses atomic_write. Inherited for free.
- Modes #2, #3, #5 unchanged (eventually consistent on next sync).

Deferred to follow-up PRs:
- 13 remaining SharedWriter mutations (create/update/close/reopen/delete issue, comments, milestones, set/clear milestone, intervention comments, create_subissue). Each is a similar ~10-line rewire once any missing event variants are added (CommentAdded, MilestoneCreated/Closed/Deleted, IssueDeleted).
- Migration tool to synthesize events for legacy JSON-only issues.
- integrity hydration --repair becomes 'wipe checkpoint + recompact from genesis' (currently still uses the JSON-aware logic from #602).
- Removing write_commit_push entirely.

Tests: 2879 pass (+1 new — test_add_blocker_writes_dependency_event_to_log asserts the event log actually receives the envelope). All 21 existing tests for the rewired mutations still pass. Clippy -D warnings -W unwrap_used -W expect_used clean. Fmt clean.

e618b81e-cb6f-47f2-86f0-84f47e1c2d06.title: Fix claim_lock and release_lock ignoring V2 layout

1f8e1411-28c4-4470-9fef-af6720caaeb6.author: anon-ab6221f8
1f8e1411-28c4-4470-9fef-af6720caaeb6.content: On closer inspection, all callers already dispatch V1 vs V2 before reaching sync.claim_lock/release_lock. The V1 functions are only called on V1 layouts. The issue was that the functions themselves don't enforce this — they silently do the wrong thing if called on V2. Adding a V2 guard that bails with a clear error instead of silently writing to the wrong file.

478e42a5-a145-4ba5-a467-615ba1d3520f.author: anon-ab6221f8
478e42a5-a145-4ba5-a467-615ba1d3520f.content: Add V2 layout dispatch in claim_lock and release_lock. When is_v2_layout(), delegate to the SharedWriter V2 methods instead of writing to V1 locks.json.

f038c1f6-dc54-49e2-9f2a-abb1f8f19832.content: Added tracing::warn guards to claim_lock and release_lock when called on V2 layouts. All callers already dispatch V1/V2 correctly, but these guards surface misuse via logs instead of silently writing to wrong file. Bail was too strict (broke tests). All 1665 tests pass.

5ed912f9-c945-4b3f-905b-b3f5fb5f4455.author: dashboard-bootstrap
5ed912f9-c945-4b3f-905b-b3f5fb5f4455.content: Result: committed 2ec6ecd5. New file crosslink/Cross.toml (+31 lines). Pre-build directive creates /dashboard/dist/ with 0777 perms inside the cross container's filesystem so build.rs can write the dashboard placeholder there at cargo-build time, satisfying rust-embed's compile-time folder check. Branch state: 4 commits ahead of develop.

6f91c590-811b-4fbf-8a53-d5b78f9e3de9.author: dashboard-bootstrap
6f91c590-811b-4fbf-8a53-d5b78f9e3de9.content: Plan: cross build now compiles past the libsqlite3-sys link failure but hits a new error — rust-embed's compile-time check for ../dashboard/dist/ fails because cross mounts only the crate dir (crosslink/) as /project, putting ../dashboard/ outside the mount. build.rs's placeholder-write fallback also fails (different filesystem perms inside the cross container). Fix: add crosslink/Cross.toml with a pre-build directive that creates /dashboard/dist/ with permissive perms inside the cross container's filesystem. cross's pre-build runs at image-build time and the result persists in the cross-image, so build.rs (which then runs at cargo-build time inside that image) can drop its placeholder index.html into the now-writable directory and rust-embed sees it at compile time. Single new file. Surgical to the cross-only path; no impact on native amd64 build.

9726ef9b-9458-4c5a-ab42-b7f25a024d83.author: dashboard-bootstrap
9726ef9b-9458-4c5a-ab42-b7f25a024d83.content: Plan: arm64 musl cross-compile failed in CI as predicted in the audit. Concrete error: liblibsqlite3_sys (built from sqlite3.c via the build script) emits glibc-only symbols (open64/stat64/fstat64/ftruncate64/fcntl64/pread64/pwrite64/mmap64/lstat64 — Large File Support; __memcpy_chk/__memset_chk/__memmove_chk — _FORTIFY_SOURCE) because gcc-aarch64-linux-gnu is a glibc cross-compiler. The musl libc has none of those, so ld fails with 'undefined reference'. Fix: switch the arm64 leg to cross-rs/cross which runs cargo inside a docker image carrying a proper aarch64-musl toolchain. Keep amd64 native (musl-tools work fine — only the cross direction needs cross-rs). Install via taiki-e/install-action (fast — fetches prebuilt binary instead of cargo install). Single workflow file edit. Commit on the same branch.

a0d5f1ed-ceae-48cf-8996-b8087eaedcb3.author: dashboard-bootstrap
a0d5f1ed-ceae-48cf-8996-b8087eaedcb3.content: Plan: re-point crosslink container build (and start/snapshot/staleness check, since they share the same constants) to compose with kickoff. Concrete: change IMAGE_NAME from 'crosslink-agent' to 'ghcr.io/forecast-bio/crosslink-agent' so all container.rs subcommands look up + produce the same image name kickoff defaults to. Change build()'s default --tag from IMAGE_TAG ('latest') to a new BUILD_DEFAULT_TAG ('local') so a local rebuild doesn't accidentally clobber a pulled :latest. Update the stale-image warning text to reference docker pull / just build-image rather than just 'crosslink container build'. Update CLI help on the --tag flag. Add a unit test asserting the GHCR-namespaced image name is used. Single follow-up commit on the same feature branch.

edbff58a-dc9f-484b-9ea7-8529b3f0c94d.title: Add clock skew detection using git commit timestamps as witness

10af4e1f-7330-4fa3-bbcb-671d10098eca.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--clock-skew
10af4e1f-7330-4fa3-bbcb-671d10098eca.content: PreToolUse hook blocked Write because session had no active issue despite earlier 'session work 114' call. Re-ran 'session work 114' to re-activate.

55d20196-156e-4f39-b7d9-4dc5892c9e8b.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--clock-skew
55d20196-156e-4f39-b7d9-4dc5892c9e8b.content:
Plan: The compaction infrastructure (GH #119) is already fully implemented in compaction.rs. The existing detect_clock_skew() compares event timestamps against Utc::now(), which is a basic sanity check. The new feature adds a more robust check using git commit timestamps as an independent witness - the git commit that introduced an event to the hub branch serves as a trusted timestamp oracle.

Approach:
1. Create new clock_skew.rs module with SkewViolation struct and git-based detection
2. For each agent's events.log, use 'git log' to find commits that modified it
3. For each commit, parse the diff to find added event lines
4. Compare event timestamps against GIT_COMMITTER_DATE
5. Flag violations where |event_timestamp - commit_timestamp| > 60s
6. Write violations to checkpoint/skew_warnings.json (separate from state.json)
7. Integrate into compact() function and log warnings in CLI output
8. Add unit tests using temp git repos with controlled commit timestamps

Key files to modify: lib.rs, compaction.rs, main.rs
Key files to create: clock_skew.rs

fa33c72f-fe95-4ed1-a79e-c2fb62b1acc9.title: Restructure CI/CD workflows to reduce duplication and optimize minute usage

03df622e-1038-4ecb-95a0-e0f622d7cf9f.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure
03df622e-1038-4ecb-95a0-e0f622d7cf9f.content: Decision: ci.yml triggers on push/PR to develop (not main). PRs to main are exclusively handled by publish.yml which calls ci.yml via workflow_call. This eliminates the duplicate CI run where both ci.yml and publish.yml would trigger on the same PR to main.

08e4567c-a424-4123-b46e-036fbbd20259.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure
08e4567c-a424-4123-b46e-036fbbd20259.content: Decision: Removed build-release job from ci.yml and created separate release-builds.yml. Rationale: release binaries (3 platforms including Windows) are expensive and only needed for actual releases — no reason to build them on every PR to develop. This saves ~15 min of CI per develop PR.

106cbbd8-b9aa-47f3-bcae-b344975a610a.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure
106cbbd8-b9aa-47f3-bcae-b344975a610a.content: Decision: Added concurrency groups to ci.yml, ci-feature.yml, and release-builds.yml to cancel stale runs. Using PR number for PR events and ref for push events to avoid cross-cancellation between PR validation and post-merge runs.

226bcf7c-275f-43ea-bbb5-9c2d26c27a7f.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure
226bcf7c-275f-43ea-bbb5-9c2d26c27a7f.content: Observation: crosslink session work state keeps resetting between tool calls — the session focus does not persist across hook checks. Working around by re-calling session work before each gated operation.

3459bce9-26f6-42c4-bad5-811693382c04.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure
3459bce9-26f6-42c4-bad5-811693382c04.content:
Plan: Restructure CI/CD workflows for git flow branching model (GH #129).

Current state:
- ci.yml: push main, PR main, workflow_call → lint, security, test (2 OS), proptest, fuzz, build-release (3 OS)
- publish.yml: tag v*, PR main (paths filter) → calls ci.yml then dry-run or publish
- docs.yml: push main (docs_src paths) → build + deploy pages

Problems: duplicate CI on tag push (publish calls ci.yml + ci.yml triggers independently), build-release runs on every CI invocation even PRs, no tiering by branch type.

Proposed git-flow-aware structure (4 workflow files):

1. ci.yml — Full validation suite
   Triggers: push develop, PR develop, workflow_call
   Jobs: lint, security, test (2 OS), proptest, fuzz
   Note: build-release REMOVED from ci.yml (moved to release-builds.yml)
   Concurrency group to cancel stale runs.

2. ci-feature.yml — Fast feature branch CI (NEW)
   Triggers: push feature/**
   Jobs: build + unit tests + integration tests (ubuntu only)
   Target: <2 min feedback loop for agent iteration.

3. publish.yml — Crates.io release pipeline
   Triggers: tag v*, PR main
   Jobs: calls ci.yml (workflow_call) + dry-run (on PR) or publish (on tag)
   Removed paths filter — in git flow, all PRs to main are releases.

4. release-builds.yml — Binary artifact builds (NEW)
   Triggers: tag v*, PR main
   Jobs: build release binaries (linux, macos, windows), upload artifacts
   Separated from CI so feature/develop validation skips expensive cross-platform release builds.

5. docs.yml — Unchanged (push main + docs_src paths + workflow_dispatch)

Trigger matrix (no duplicates):
  feature/* push     → ci-feature.yml only (fast)
  PR → develop       → ci.yml (full suite, no release builds)
  push develop       → ci.yml (safety net for merge result)
  PR → main          → publish.yml → ci.yml + dry-run + release-builds.yml
  tag v* push        → publish.yml → ci.yml + publish + release-builds.yml
  push main (docs)   → docs.yml

Estimated savings:
- Feature branches: ~80% reduction (skip lint, security, proptest, fuzz, release builds, macOS)
- PR to develop: ~30% reduction (skip 3-platform release builds)
- Eliminated duplicate CI on releases

1. ci.yml — Full validation suite
   Triggers: push develop, PR develop, workflow_call
   Jobs: lint, security, test (2 OS), proptest, fuzz
   Note: build-release REMOVED from ci.yml (moved to release-builds.yml)
   Concurrency group to cancel stale runs.

2. ci-feature.yml — Fast feature branch CI (NEW)
   Triggers: push feature/**
   Jobs: build + unit tests + integration tests (ubuntu only)
   Target: <2 min feedback loop for agent iteration.

3. publish.yml — Crates.io release pipeline
   Triggers: tag v*, PR main
   Jobs: calls ci.yml (workflow_call) + dry-run (on PR) or publish (on tag)
   Removed paths filter — in git flow, all PRs to main are releases.

4. release-builds.yml — Binary artifact builds (NEW)
   Triggers: tag v*, PR main
   Jobs: build release binaries (linux, macos, windows), upload artifacts
   Separated from CI so feature/develop validation skips expensive cross-platform release builds.

5. docs.yml — Unchanged (push main + docs_src paths + workflow_dispatch)

Trigger matrix (no duplicates):
  feature/* push     → ci-feature.yml only (fast)
  PR → develop       → ci.yml (full suite, no release builds)
  push develop       → ci.yml (safety net for merge result)
  PR → main          → publish.yml → ci.yml + dry-run + release-builds.yml
  tag v* push        → publish.yml → ci.yml + publish + release-builds.yml
  push main (docs)   → docs.yml

Estimated savings:
- Feature branches: ~80% reduction (skip lint, security, proptest, fuzz, release builds, macOS)
- PR to develop: ~30% reduction (skip 3-platform release builds)
- Eliminated duplicate CI on releases

47f0e8d7-05c0-40ab-bf3b-f7e76b3778aa.author: maxine-at-forecast--noether--ci-cd-restructure

This eliminates the duplicate CI run where both ci.yml and publish.yml would trigger on the same PR to main.

fabce6a0-e7b3-4234-a869-bc393a86fca6.title: Dashboard: project detail page blank on click + gh CLI auth fallback

5f2655aa-0a65-4e8c-8d31-877249e40314.author: anon-ab6221f8
5f2655aa-0a65-4e8c-8d31-877249e40314.content:
Root cause: std::fs::read("/dev/urandom") in github.rs::derive_machine_key + github.rs::seal. std::fs::read internally does read_to_end; /dev/urandom has NO EOF so the Vec grew forever. Running cargo test --lib dashboard::github fires 5 tests in parallel, each calling seal(), producing 5 simultaneous infinite reads → ~32 GB OOM → WSL crash. Found by parallel subagent audit (agent 4, opus 4.7).

Fix in github.rs: File::open + read_exact(&mut buf) for both sites. Nonce path now propagates errors instead of falling through to all-zeros (a zero AES-GCM nonce with key reuse is catastrophic).

Verified with ulimit -v 8388608 (8 GB cap): 5 github tests run in 0.01s. Previously OOM'd WSL.

Also fixed in same commit:
• Blank project page — IssueFile backend skip_serializing_if=Vec::is_empty omits labels/blockers/related when empty; frontend declared them required, crashed on .length. Marked optional + defensive ?? [] at accesses.
• gh CLI fallback — new TokenSource enum + gh_cli_token() + get_effective_token() resolver. UI now auto-picks up `gh auth login` credentials when no PAT is stored; config endpoint returns token_source: 'stored' | 'gh-cli' | null so the UI knows.

Commit: 166a8aba

fd51a35b-3ed1-4dd3-a59d-5bda2e0e95f9.title: Fix smoke-verify auth + ensure GHCR package is publicly pullable (post-merge of #578)

aff49684-d99c-4da6-9061-786805bda451.author: dashboard-bootstrap
aff49684-d99c-4da6-9061-786805bda451.content: Plan: two-part fix. (1) smoke-verify job needs a docker login step before docker pull — currently only publish-image authenticates with GITHUB_TOKEN, so smoke-verify hits 'unauthorized' against the just-pushed (private-by-default) GHCR package. Adding the same docker/login-action@v3 step that publish-image uses. (2) The deeper issue: GH#576's whole point was 'users can pull without manual auth' — but GHCR creates packages as private on first publish, and the only way to make them public is a manual admin action via the package settings page. CI fix unblocks the workflow; the manual step unblocks the actual user-facing feature. Documenting the manual step in the PR body + adding a CHANGELOG note + considering whether to add a one-time bootstrap script. Need to verify whether linking the package to the repo (which happens automatically on first push) inherits the repo's visibility — I think it does for new packages, but the existing :nightly was published before any visibility setting was made, so it may need explicit toggling.

ed58961c-cf35-44dc-96b7-07c45daee1b7.author: dashboard-bootstrap
ed58961c-cf35-44dc-96b7-07c45daee1b7.content: Result: committed a3582686 on fix/728-smoke-verify-auth-and-ghcr-public. Single workflow file edit (+13 lines). Adds docker/login-action@v3 step to smoke-verify before the docker pull, mirroring publish-image's login. Inline comment explains WHY (GHCR private-by-default) so it doesn't get deleted as 'redundant' once the package is public. Commit signed. Ready for user to push and PR to develop. IMPORTANT separate manual step: the package itself is still private (CI fix doesn't change that — only fixes the workflow's ability to verify itself). User-facing pull from outside the repo's auth scope still fails until an admin flips package visibility to public.
